Upgrade (Level 11): Further increases Dragonite's movement speed by 10% for 4.5s if there are enemies nearby when the dance ends.

moves.name: Extreme Speed
moves.description: Dash toward the designated location stopping upon contact with an enemy, dealing damage and throwing them.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Dash toward the designated enemy, damaging and slowing all enemies in a small radius around the target upon impact by 50% for 2s, throwing the primary target and shoving others away.  Using this move resets the cooldown of Multiscale.

Upgrade (Level 11): If Dragonite's movement speed is currently decreased when this move is used, that movement speed decrease is negated before the move is used.

moves.name: Dragon Breath
moves.description: Exhale a mighty gust in the designated direction. If this move hits an enemy, the user's next attack becomes a boosted attack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Exhale a mighty gust in the designated direction. If this move hits an enemy, Dragonite's next attack becomes a boosted attack.

moves.name: Hyper Beam
moves.description:
Fires a beam of energy toward the targeted enemy after a delay, dealing damage to all enemies that it hits, as well as additional damage based on their Max HP. The move changes Stages based on the number of stacks of Dragon Dance that Dragonite has, from 0 to 3. The Max HP damage is 5%/10%/15%/20%, determined by the current Stage.  Dragonite cannot act during the duration of this move.

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduces damage taken by 50% while charging this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Fires a beam of energy toward the targeted enemy after a delay, dealing damage to all enemies that it hits, as well as additional damage based on their Max HP. The move changes Stages based on the number of stacks of Dragon Dance that Dragonite has, from 0 to 3. The max HP damage is 0%/6%/12%/18%, determined by the current Stage. Dragonite cannot act while releasing the beam.

Against Wild Pokémon: The additional damage from max HP is capped at 600/1200/1800/2400 depending on the stage.

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduces damage taken by 50% while charging this move.

moves.name: Outrage
moves.description: Dragonite begins rampaging for 5s. This move starts with a ground stomp, dealing damage to enemies in an area around Dragonite. While rampaging, all of Dragonite's basic attacks become a melee boosted attack, alternating through the water, electric, and fire boosted attack types. When the rampage ends, Dragonite is left briefly unable to act.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Dragonite stomps the ground, dealing damage to enemies in an area around Dragonite with the bonus effect of the last Boosted Attack that was used. Additionally, Dragonite gains a buff for 5.5s, increasing auto attack speed by 35% for 5.5s, converting all attacks during the duration of Outrage into melee Boosted Attacks, and alternating through the water, electric, and fire Boosted Attacks in order. When the buff ends, Dragonite is stunned for .7s.

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduce the duration of hindrances inflicted on Dragonite while rampaging by 40% for 5.5s.

moves.name: Draco Impact
moves.description: Briefly fly up in the air before crashing down with several comets on a designated area, dealing damage and throwing enemies in the area. The farther Dragonite flies, the more the Unite guage is consumed (25% at min disatance and 100% at max). Dragonite is unstoppable while using this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Briefly fly up in the air, becoming untargetable, before crashing down, approximately 2s after activating, with several comets on a designated area, dealing damage and throwing enemies in the area for 0.5s. The farther Dragonite flies, the more the Unite gauge is consumed (50% at min distance and 100% at max). Dragonite is unstoppable while using this move.

passiveAbility.name: Marvel Scale
passiveAbility.description: Increases Defense by 100 for 1.5s when afflicted by a status condition (Dratini and Dragonair only).

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, shooting a long beam that deals a large amount of damage to all enemies hit.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, shooting a long beam that deals a large amount of damage to all enemies hit.

moves.name: Laser Focus
moves.description: Reduces damage received for a short time. Afterward, Duraludon's basic attacks deal additional damage to enemies hit. This buff expires after 8s or after 3 basic attacks.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Reduces damage received by 20% for .6s. Afterward, Duraludon's auto attacks deal additional damage to enemies hit. This buff expires after 8s or after 3 auto attacks.

moves.name: Flash Cannon
moves.description:
Duraludon ruptures the ground around themself and in a designated direction; dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed by 50% for 1s. Afterward, go into "Cannon Mode" rooting Duraludon for 6s or if a basic attack is not made for 2.5s or this move can be cancelled early. While in Cannon Mode, basic attacks have longer range, attack speed is increased by 100% but decays by 20% per second over 5 seconds, dealing increased damage and splash damage. Exiting "Cannon mode" will prematurely remove the attack speed buff. Metal Claw, Stealth Rock, Dragon Tail, and boosted attacks can be used while in this mode and boosted attacks decrease the movement speed of enemies hit by 60% for 1s and also pierce.

Upgrade (Level 11): Enemies in the ruptured ground's area of effect are also unable to act for 1s.

moves.name: Dragon Pulse
moves.description:
Charges power before dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect. Duraludon's movement speed is decreased while charging, but the longer power is charged, the wider the move's area of effect becomes and the more damage it deals. After learning this move, a damage marker will be attached to any enemy Duraludon damages. When Dragon Pulse hits a marked enemy, it deals additional damage to that target for each damage marker attached to it, then removes all of the damage markers. In addition, if this move knocks out an enemy, the cooldown is reduced by 50% and the next 3 basic attacks will each deal additional damage and further reduce this move's cooldown when they hit.

Upgrade (Level 11): Also restores Duraludon's HP if this move knocks out an enemy player.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Duraludon begins charging power, slowing itself by 30%. The longer the move is charged the greater the damage and area of effect. After learning this move a mark will be applied to any enemy Duraludon damages with auto attacks and moves. Each tick of damage from Duraludon (boosted attack counts as 1 instance) will apply 1 mark up to a maximum of 5 orbs and 1 ring. This mark lasts for 5s, is refreshed when Duraludon deals damage to the marked enemy and can be cleansed. When Dragon Pulse hits a marked enemy, the marks are consumed and additional damage is dealt for each mark. Opposing Duraludon's can consume the user's marks but will not gain the additional damage. Furthermore, if this move knocks out an enemy, the cooldown is reduced by 30% and Duraludon receives a buff for 8s or 3 auto attacks. While this buff is active, auto attacks deal additional damage and reduce this moves cooldown by 1.5s each.

Upgrade (Level 11): Also restores 8% of Duraludon's max HP if this move knocks out an enemy player.

moves.name: Metal Claw
moves.description: Strike with sharp claws; dealing damage to enemies hit and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. After this move is used, Duraludon's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Send sharp claws forward; dealing damage to enemies hit and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 2s. After this move is used, Duraludon's next auto attack becomes a boosted attack.

moves.name: Dragon Tail
moves.description:
Duraludon shoves enemies and moves itself backward a short distance. The next basic attack becomes a boosted attack. A maximum of two uses can be kept in reserve for this move. There is a 1.1s cooldown between uses. This move can penetrate through walls.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increased damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Duraludon shoves enemies for 0.5s and moves itself backward a short distance. The next auto attack becomes a boosted attack. This move ignores obstacles. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve for this move. There is a 2.5s cooldown between uses.

Upgrade (Level 13): Apply a mark for 5s that is consumed the next time Duraludon damages them, dealing additional damage.

moves.name: Stealth Rock
moves.description:
Hurl a stone at the designated area, creating an invisible field of stones. The field deals damage over time to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their movement speed for a short time. If the hurled stone hits an enemy before the designated area, the area of effect will instead be centered on where the stone hit that Pokémon. Once an enemy takes damage from the field, it will become visible to enemies.  If an enemy takes damage from this move 4 times, that enemy becomes unable to move for a short time. After this move is used, Duraludon's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack. A maximum of two uses can be kept in reserve for this move. There is a 1.1s cooldown between uses.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the maximum number of uses that can be kept in reserve for this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Hurl a stone at the designated area, creating an invisible field of stones for 60s or until triggered. The field deals damage over time to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their movement speed by 25% for 2s, stacking up to 4 times. If the hurled stone hits an enemy before the designated area, the field will instead be centered on where the stone hit that Pokémon. Once an enemy takes damage from the field, it will become visible to all enemies. When the field becomes visible this way, Duraludon is granted a shield for 3s, can stack a maximum of 5 times, and the duration is refreshed upon gaining a stack. If an enemy takes damage from this move 2 times, that enemy becomes unable to move for 1s. After this move is used, Duraludon's next auto attack becomes a boosted attack. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve for this move. There is a 1s cooldown between uses.

A maximum of 2 Stealth Rocks may be on the game field at one time, but 2 more can still be kept in reserve.

Upgrade (Level 13): Stores 3 uses in reserve instead.

moves.name: Revolving Ruin
moves.description: Fire a beam of light at the ground in a sweeping circle around Duraludon. If this beam hits an enemy, it deals damage to them, grants Duraludon a shield and increases their Attack by 8% for a short time. Both the shield effect and the Attack increase are strengthened for each enemy that is hit by the beam. Damage is dealt to any enemy in the area of effect after the beam of light is fired. A burning ring is also left where the beam of light touched the ground, and enemies that touch this ring take additional damage and have their movement speed decreased by 50% for 1s refreshing as damage continus to be taken from the burning ground.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Fire a beam of light at the ground in a sweeping circle around Duraludon, becoming unstoppable during the rotation. If this beam hits an enemy, it deals damage to them, grants Duraludon a shield and increases Attack by 8% for 8s. Both the shield effect and the Attack increase are strengthened for each enemy that is hit by the beam, stacking up to 5 times. Damage is dealt to any enemy in the area of effect after the beam of light is fired and enemies are shoved for 0.5s. A burning ring is also left where the beam of light touched the ground for 10s; enemies that touch this ring take additional damage every 0.5s and have their movement speed decreased by 50% for 1s. Subsequent hits after the first have the Attack stat bonus from this move applied to them.

passiveAbility.name: Heavy Metal
passiveAbility.description: Prevents Duraludon from being thrown or shoved by the first attempt to shove or throw them. This ability goes on cooldown after it's triggered (50s cd).
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: Prevents Duraludon from being thrown or shoved by the first attempt to throw or shove them. This ability goes on cooldown after it's triggered (50s cd).

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, attaching a bit of cotton fluff to the enemy. The user's basic attacks against an enemy with cotton fluff on it deal increased damage and decreases the enemies movement speed for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, attaching a bit of cotton fluff to opposing Pokémon for 3s and then damaging them. The user and their teammates' auto attacks against targets with cotton fluff on them deal additional damage and decrease their movement speed by 20% for 1s.

Each teammate uses their own Sp. Atk to deal additional damage on targets with cotton fluff. This additional damage has an internal cooldown of 0.35s for each teammate.

moves.name: Leafage
moves.description: Pelt the target with leaves, decreasing the movement speed of enemies for a short time when it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user shoots a bundle of leaves in the designated direction which explodes on the first opposing Pokémon hit or after traveling max distance, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed by 40% for 2s.

moves.name: Pollen Puff
moves.description:
Throws a Pollen Puff, which attaches to an enemy. The Pollen Puff restores HP for allies and deals damage to enemies.

Upgrade (Level 10): If a Pollen Puff attaches to enemies, it also deals them damage over time. If a Pollen Puff attaches to an ally, it also reduces the damage they receive.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user throws a Pollen Puff at the designated location which attaches to the nearest Pokémon within 0.5s. The Pollen Puff explodes 2s after attaching to a Pokémon, restoring the HP of the user's teammates and damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect.

The Pollen Puff explodes on its own after 1s with the same effects if it doesn't attach to any Pokémon within the first 0.5s of landing.

Upgrade (Level 10): If the Pollen Puff attaches to a teammate, it reduces the damage that teammate receives by 15% for 2s before it explodes. If the Pollen Puff attaches to any Pokémon, opposing Pokémon that come in contact with the target receive damage over time every 0.5s before the Pollen Puff explodes.

moves.name: Leaf Tornado
moves.description:
Attack with a tornado of sharp leaves, leaving a path in its wake that increases ally movement speed for a short time.

Upgrade (Level 10): Also decreases the accuracy of enemies for a short time when this move hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user launches a tornado of sharp leaves in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon hit by the tornado and creating a path in its wake that lasts for 4s. Teammates on the path have their movement speed increased by 55%, lingering for 1.5s after leaving the path. When the tornado reaches max distance, the tornado damages opposing Pokémon a second time.

25% of the damage of the first hit.

Upgrade (Level 10): Applies a debuff to opposing Pokémon hit by the tornado that causes auto attacks to deal no damage for 2.5s.

moves.name: Synthesis
moves.description: Restores the HP of the user and nearby allies.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user uses sunlight to heal themself and teammates in the area of effect.

moves.name: Cotton Guard
moves.description:
Protects the user and nearby allies with cotton. The cotton absorbs damage and when the move ends, the cotton restores some HP.

Upgrade (Level 12): Also increases the user's movement speed while using this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user grants teammates in the area of effect cotton shields for 3s. After 3s, any cotton leftover is used to restore the HP of teammates with cotton shields for 15% of the remaining shield value.

Upgrade (Level 12): Increases the movement speed of teammates in the area of effect by 30% for 2s.

moves.name: Cotton Spore
moves.description:
Gathers spores around the user, increasing the user's movement speed while this move is in effect. The spores burst after a short time, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect, throwing them, and decreasing their movement speed for a short time.

Upgrade (Level 12): Reduced damage taken while using this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user gathers spores around them for 2s, increasing their movement speed by 40% for 2s. The spores burst at the end, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect, throwing them for 0.5s and decreasing their movement speed by 40% for 2s when it hits.

Cotton Cloud Crash can be used during this move, ending the move early and making the spores burst immediately.

Upgrade (Level 12): Increases the user's Defense and Sp. Def by 50% for 2s.

moves.name: Cotton Cloud Crash
moves.description: Float into the air and become invincible. If used again, pound the ground, attacking and shoving enemies and restoring HP to allies in the area of effect.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user becomes invincible as they float into the air for up to 3s, moving freely over obstacles and increasing their movement speed by 10% while in the air. If used again or after 3s, the user pounds the ground, restoring the HP of the user and teammates in the area of effect, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and shoving opposing Pokémon hit for 0.7s.

passiveAbility.name: Cotton Down
passiveAbility.description: Damages an enemy and decreases its movement speed for a short time after receiving a certain amount of damage. When that happens, also recover HP and gain increased movement speed for a short time.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: When at least 5% max HP damage is received in a single attack, restores this Pokémon's HP and increases their movement speed by 15% for 1.5s; deals revenge damage to nearby opposing Pokémon and decreases their movement speed by 30% for 1.5s (10s CD).

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Piplup shoots a blast of water, becoming a boosted attack with every third attack dealing increased damage. Basic and Boosted attacks increase Torrent gauge by 2/3/4.

When evolved into Prinplup or Empoleon, basic attacks become melee ranged. The user slices with their wings, becoming a boosted attack with every third attack dealing increased damage. Boosted attack increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20. This Pokémon heals 15% of damage dealt with basic and boosted attacks.

moves.name: Water Gun
moves.description: Has the user blast a forceful shot of water in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokmon it hits. When this move is boosted by Torrent, it deals increased damage. (Torrent gauge consumed: 50).
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user blasts a forceful shot of water in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move deals increased damage.

moves.name: Hydro Cannon
moves.description: Has the user launch a watery blast in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and leaving them unable to act. When this move is boosted by Torrent, a whirlpool appears and deals damage over time. The user also recovers HP when in contact with the Whirlpool. (Torrent gauge consumed: 50).
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user launches a watery blast in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and stunning them for 0.5s. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move leaves behind a whirlpool for 3s which deals damage to opposing Pokémon, and heals the user when they stand in it, every 0.5s.

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduces this moves cooldown by 30% of the remaining cooldown when it hits Pokémon from the opposing team.

moves.name: Whirlpool
moves.description: Has the user create a violent, swirling whirlpool around itself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and restoring the user's HP. When this move is boosted by Torrent, the amount of damage dealt and HP restored are increased, and the move also throws opposing Pokémon before ending with a slash, dealing damage. (Torrent gauge consumed: 50).
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user becomes unstoppable for 1s as they create a violent swirling whirlpool around themself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon every 0.25s up to 4 hits. The user's HP is restored by 40% of the damage dealt against Pokémon on the opposing team, and by 20% against wild Pokémon. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move will throw opposing Pokémon for 0.6s and the user will slash once at the end of the move in the designated direction extending the unstoppable duration to 1.33s. The healing of the move is increased to 50% of the damage dealt against Pokémon on the opposing team, and 25% against wild Pokémon.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increased damage.

moves.name: Peck
moves.description: Has the user jab its sharply pointed beak in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. When this move is boosted by Torrent, it applies a slowing effect (Torrent gauge consumed: 50).
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user jabs its sharply pointed beak in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move applies a 40% slow for 2s.

moves.name: Metal Claw
moves.description: Has the user charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokmon with wings of steel and shoving them. When this move is boosted by Torrent, the user receives a shield effect. (Torrent gauge consumed: 50).
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Has the user charge towards a designated target, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon with wings of steel and shoving them for 0.25s. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move grants the user a shield for 3s. This shield will not stack with itself.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increased the shield from Torrent.

moves.name: Aqua Jet
moves.description: Has the user lunge at blinding speed in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and increasing the user's basic attack speed for a short time. When this move is boosted by Torrent, it decreases the Sp. Def of Pokémon from the opposing team. (Torrent gauge consumed: 50).
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Has the user lunge at blinding speed in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and increasing the user's basic attack speed by 40% for 5s. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move will also lower the special defense of those hit by 60% for 3s.

Upgrade (Level 11): Lowers the base cooldown of this move by 1s.

moves.name: Sovereign Slide
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Has the user slide at high speed in the designated direction and form a whirlpool on impact, or when the maximum distance is reached, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and throwing them for 1s. The whirlpool remains centered on the user for 7s and applies a 50% slow to opposing Pokémon in the area. The slow lasts 1s when opposing Pokémon leave the area. Empoleon is unstoppable throughout this move. The longer the user slides, the faster the recovery to perform other actions.

Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when the move connects. While the whirlpool is active the amount of Torrent gauge gained per second is increased to 12/18/24.

passiveAbility.name: Torrent
passiveAbility.description: When this Pokémon deals damage to opposing Pokémon, or as time passes, the Torrent gauge increases to a max of 100. If a move is used when the Torrent gauge exceeds 50, 50 points of the gauge is consumed and the move becomes boosted.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
When this Pokémon deals damage to opposing Pokémon, or as time passes, the Torrent gauge increases to a max of 100. If a move is used when the Torrent gauge exceeds 50, 50 points of the gauge is consumed and the move becomes boosted.

The rate at which the Torrent gauge increases depends on your current HP with thresholds at: above 66% HP, below 66% HP, and below 33% HP. Every second gain 1/2/3 Torrent gauge.

moves.name: Attack - Eevee
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage.

moves.name: Swift
moves.description: Shoots star-shaped rays at opposing Pokémon, dealing damage to them.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Eevee launches three stars at target location. Stars start in a horizontal line perpendicular to Eevee's facing. Stars continue to fly in the direction once past the target location until the maximum range is reached. When an enemy is hit by a star, subsequent stars deal 70% less damage.

moves.name: Psyshock
moves.description: Has the user materialize psychic projectiles and hurl them at a designated location, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit by the projectiles and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. If an opposing Pokémon is hit by all of these psychic projectiles, that Pokémon is left unable to act for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Espeon materializes psychic projectiles and launches them at a designated location; dealing damage to enemies hit by the projectiles and decreasing their movement speed for each projectile hit by 15% for 3s (maximum 75%). Orbs start in a horizontal line perpendicular to Espeon's facing and continue flying in the  designated direction until the maximum range of the move. Targets hit by all five projectiles are stunned for 1.25s. When an enemy is hit by a projectile, subsequent projectiles deal 70% less damage. When Psyshock is enhanced via Future Sight reset, it launches stronger psychic projectiles at a designated location. These projectiles at their point of convergence cause an explosion, dealing damage to enemies in the area-of-effect. There is no damage increase.

Upgrade (Level 10): Increase the speed at which projectiles travel.

moves.name: Stored Power
moves.description: Has the user unleash blasts of psychic power at random opposing Pokémon in the area of effect, dealing damage to them and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. The user stores one blast of psychic power whenever a set amount of time passes or after the user hits an opposing Pokémon with a boosted basic attack. Up to five blasts of psychic power can be stored at once. After this move is used, the user's movement speed increases for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Unleash blasts of psychic power at random enemies within an area; dealing damage to enemies hit and decreasing their movement speed from each projectile hit by 10% for 2s (maximum 50%). Espeon stores one blast of psychic power every 2s (unaffected by cooldown reduction), or after hitting an enemy with a boosted attack (gaining 1 stored blast per enemy hit). Up to five blasts can be stored at once. After this move is used, Espeon's movement speed is increased by 70% for 1.2s. Targets hit by a psychic blast will take 70% less damage from subsequent hits. When Stored Power is enhanced via Future Sight reset, it unleashes an increased number of psychic blasts (8 total) and restores Espeon's HP by 150 per blast.

Upgrade (Level 10): Also deals additional damage to enemies below 50% max HP.

moves.name: Growl
moves.description: Has the user growl at a designated opposing Pokémon, dealing damage and decreasing its movement speed for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Growl at a designated enemy; dealing damage and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 2s.

moves.name: Psybeam
moves.description: Has the user fire a peculiar ray in the designated direction. If the ray hits an opposing Pokémon, it will then split beyond the first Pokémon and deal damage to other opposing Pokémon in range. These split rays deal more damage the higher the max HP of the first Pokémon hit by the ray. Furthermore, if the first Pokémon hit by the ray has its movement speed decreased or unable to act, those same effects will be applied to the Pokémon hit by the split rays.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Fires a peculiar ray in the designated direction. If the beam hits an enemy, it will then split beyond the first enemy hit and deal damage to other enemies in range. The initial enemy hit and these split rays deal more damage based on the max HP of the first enemy hit by the ray. If the first enemy hit by the ray is slowed or is stunned, those same effects will be applied to the enemies hit by the split rays. The split rays follow Espeons relative facing direction and will refract at a maximum of a 90 degree angle. Distance from initial target to split ray hit is 75% of the max range. Stun copied duration is the maximum duration of the stun causing the initial Pokémon to be stunned.

6% max HP of the first enemy hit as additional damage.

For the split ray, the damage component from the initial target's max HP damage is capped at 1000 against Wild Pokémon and capped at 2000 against Player Pokémon.

Upgrade (Level 12): Deals additional damage to enemies that are unable to act. If Psybeam hits an unable to act initial target, the split rays will also deal the additional damage to other enemies hit and leaves them unable to act.

moves.name: Future Sight
moves.description: Has the user lock on to an opposing Pokémon, then deal damage a few seconds later to opposing Pokémon in an area of effect centered on the locked-on Pokémon. More damage is dealt the lower the locked-on Pokémon's remaining HP is. When the user is locked onto an opposing Pokémon, its other moves will target that Pokémon whenever possible. Each time one of those moves deals damage, Future Sight's cooldown is reduced. If the locked-on Pokémon is on the opposing team and is knocked out before the area-of-effect damage is dealt, a single powered-up use of Psyshock or Stored Power becomes available for a short time. When Psyshock is powered up, it hurls stronger psychich projectiles at a designated location that also cause an explosion, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. When Stored Power is powered up, it unleashes an increased number of power blasts and restores the user's HP when the blasts hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Espeon locks-on to an enemy, dealing damage 2 seconds later in an area centered on the locked-on target. Deal more damage based on the locked-on targets missing HP. While locked-on to an enemy, Espeon's other moves will target that enemy whenever possible. If the center of Psyshock is within the area-of-effect of Future Sight when released, Psyshock will home in on the Future Sight affected target. Each time Espeon deals damage to a Future Sight locked-on target through Psyshock, Stored Power, auto attacks, Future Sight's cooldown is reduced by 0.8 seconds. If the locked-on target is an opponent, and is knocked out by Future Sight or by Espeon, then a single powered-up use of Psyshock or Stored Power becomes available for a short time.

12% of enemy missing HP.

Full Heal and other cleanses such as Blissey's Natural cure remove Future Sight. Against Wild Pokémon: The total damage is capped at 1800.

Upgrade (Level 12): Espeon deals 15% additional damage to locked-on targets.

moves.name: Psychic Solare
moves.description: Has the user throw opposing Pokémon in the area of effect into the air and suspend them using Psychic power. After a dela, an explosion occurs that deals damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and shoves them.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Espeon throws enemies in the area of effect into the air and suspends them using psychic power. After a delay, an explosion occurs that deals damage to all enemies in the area of effect and shoves them. Espeon is unstoppable during this move.

passiveAbility.name: Anticipation
passiveAbility.description: When Eevee would be affected by a hindrance, the hindrance is negated and Eevee becomes immune to hindrances for a short time (35s cd). Note: Hindrances include slows; multi-layered hindrance moves such as Pikachu's Volt Tackle will only have the very first launch negated.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, or after the user uses Tackle, Megahorn, or Iron Head. Boosted attacks also apply a 50% slow for 1s to opposing Pokémon they hit.

When a boosted attack hits, the cooldown of Tackle, Megahorn, or Iron Head is reduced by 2.5s

moves.name: Tackle
moves.description: Has the user charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and shoving them. The user's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit and shoving them. The user's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack.

moves.name: Megahorn
moves.description: Has the user charge with its horns, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and shoving them. Immediately afterward, the user charges with its horns again and gains a shield. When the user is in No Retreat formation, it charges power before charging forward with its horns, dealing additional damage to opposing Pokémon it hits based on their max HP. When the user is in Dispatch formation, the troopers who are away from the column will also charge with their horns, along with the brass.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Charge with its horns, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and throwing them in the air for 0.25s. Immediately afterward, strike with its horns again and gain a shield for 2.5s.

6% Max HP

Trooper damage becomes additional to Column formation hitting the same target.

In No Retreat formation: Falinks becomes hindrance resistant and charges power for 1s. It then strikes forward with its horns, dealing additional damage to opposing Pokémon it hits based on their max HP and gaining a shield for 2.5s. This version of the move has a 4s cd that is separate from the column version.

Upgrade (Level 10): Increases the shield value.

moves.name: Iron Head
moves.description: Has the user attack the designated area with its steel-hard heads, dealing damage and applying a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon it hits, as well as granting itself a shield. When the user is in No Retreat formation, the move's range is shortened, but its area of attack is widened. The user's next 4 basic attacks can be used while it's moving. When the user is in Dispatch formation, dispatched troopers return to the column when the user attacks with this move. The returning troopers shove and deal damage to opposing Pokémon they hit on their way back to the column.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Attacks the designated area with its steel-hard heads, dealing damage and applying 50% slow for 1.5s to opposing Pokémon it hits, as well as granting itself a shield.

10% Max HP

In No Retreat formation: The move's range is shortened but the area of effect is widened. Pokémon hit in the landing area are also knocked up and briefly stunned for 0.33s. After using this move, for the next 4 attacks, the user can attack while moving with the Brass and Troops all deal damage separately in front of them.

Upgrade (Level 10): Iron Head and the Troopers returning deal an additional 2% max hp damage to enemies they hit, capped to 400.

moves.name: Bulk Up
moves.description: Increases the user's Attack, Defense, and basic attack speed for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Increases the user's Defense, Special Defense and Attack Speed by 25%, and Attack by 30% for 3s

moves.name: No Retreat
moves.description: Has the user switch to No Retreat formation while shoving opposing Pokémon in front of it. While in No Retreat formation, the user cannot change the direction it's facing and its movement speed when moving backward is decreased, but its Attack is increased and damage it takes from the front is reduced. If this move is used while the user is in No Retreat formation, the user will switch to Column formation.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Switch to No Retreat formation while shoving enemies for 0.4s in front of Falinks and become unstoppable during the formation change for 0.5s. While in No Retreat formation, the user cannot change the direction they're facing, unless they use the other move. Falinks' movement speed when moving backward is slowed by 10%, but Attack is increased by 25% and damage taken from the front is reduced by 20%. If this move is used while the user is in No Retreat formation, the user will switch to Column formation.

While in No Retreat formation both the Brass and Troopers take 20% less damage from the front. The Brass' total damage reduction becomes 28%; the Trooper's total damage reduction becomes 92%.

Upgrade (Level 12): Attack increase is increased to 40% and damage reduction from the front is increased to 30%.

moves.name: Beat Up
moves.description: Has the user charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. The user switches to Dispatch formation, sending out two troopers toward opposing Pokémon it hit. The dispatched troopers will continue to attack the opposing Pokémon for a while, but they will return to the brass after they take a set amount of damage or become separated from the brass by a set distance or greater. A maximum of {0} use(s) can be kept in reserve for this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Has the user charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit. The user then switches to Dispatch formation, sending out two Troopers toward enemies that were hit. The dispatched Troopers will continue to attack enemies, but they will return to the Brass after they either: take a set amount of damage, become separated from the Brass by more than a set distance, or after 5s. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve for this move with a 0.5s cooldown between each use.

While in Dispatch formation, the Troopers sent out no longer have any damage resistance nor share damage taken with the Brass. Dispatched Troopers share the same stats as the Brass, except for HP.

Upgrade (Level 12): Also restores HP when this move hits.

moves.name: Dust Devil Formation
moves.description: Has the five troopers run continuously around the brass at great speed, dealing damage and applying a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon they hit. While this move is being used, damage taken by the troopers is reduced.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Has the five troopers run continuously around the Brass at great speed for 5s, dealing damage and slowing enemies hit by 20% for 1s. Falinks is unstoppable during the formation change for 0.25s. While this move is being used, damage taken by the troopers is reduced. While in Dust Devil formation the Troopers take 30% less damage making the Trooper's total damage reduction 93%.

Moving counter to the rotation of the troopers will grant more hits against that target, and moving along the rotation will reduce the number of hits.

passiveAbility.name: Battle Armor
passiveAbility.description: The critical-hit modifier of enemies is reduced by 50% when dealing damage to Falinks. Falinks can hold 10 more Aeos Energy than other Pokémon.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
The critical-hit modifier of enemies is reduced by 50% when dealing damage to Falinks. Falinks can hold 10 more Aeos Energy than other Pokémon.

Falinks is a group comprised of the Brass and five Troopers. Each member of the group has a separate hitbox and counts as a different target. The Brass always takes 10% reduced damage. Troopers will take 90% less damage while attached to the Brass. For attacks or moves that can hit multiple targets, the total damage Falinks can take from a single move or attack is 110% of that move or attack's damage.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user slashes their claws at opposing Pokémon, dealing damage to them.

This Pokémon has no boosted attack.

moves.name: Bite
moves.description: Has the user bite with its viciously sharp fangs, dealing damage and applying a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon. The next basic attack also becomes a boosted attack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Empowers the next basic attack for up to 5s with extra damage, the user bites with its viciously sharp fangs dealing damage and slowing opposing Pokémon by 35% for 1.5s.

moves.name: Crunch
moves.description: Has the user cycle between fangs that have various effects. Using this move again selects one of the three effects, applies it to the next basic attack, and increases basic attack speed for a short time afterward. Gluttonous fangs: When a basic attack hits, the user's HP is restored. Destructive fangs: When a basic attack hits an opposing Pokémon, that Pokémon's Defense decreases. Clamping fangs: When a basic attack hits an opposing Pokémon, it applies a slowing effect.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user cycles between 3 Fangs for up to 5s. When a Fang is chosen; empowers the next basic attack with extra damage, and a variety of effects based on the chosen Fang for up to 5s. After landing the empowered basic attack, the user's attack speed increases by 140% for 5s or 3 hits. After hitting an opposing Pokémon with a Fang, a mark is left for 5s. There are three marks, one for each Fang, the more marks there are the more damage Big Jaw Bite will deal, as well as refreshing the duration of the effects from the Fangs. Future uses of this move will cycle starting from the last effect chosen.

Destructive Fangs (Yellow): deals increased damage and reduces the defense of opposing Pokémon by 30% for 5s when landing the empowered basic attack. Gluttonous Fangs (Red): after landing the empowered basic attack, for the next 5s basic attacks restore extra HP once per attack. Clamping Fangs (Purple): after landing the empowered basic attack; slows opposing Pokémon by 80% for 3s, decaying by 15% every 0.25s down to 20%.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the damage of this move.

moves.name: Scratch
moves.description: Has the user dash in the designated direction and rake opposing Pokémon with its sharp claws, dealing damage and leaving them unable to act.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user dashes in the designated direction, raking opposing Pokémon with sharp claws dealing damage and stunning them for 0.6s.

moves.name: Waterfall
moves.description: Has the user storm in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and granting the user a shield effect. When this move hits opposing Pokémon, its effect differs depending on the distance stormed. If the distance was under a certain amount, the Pokémon are left unable to act. If the distance was over a certain amount, the Pokémon are thrown.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user becomes Hindrance Resistant, gaining a shield for 3s, and begins storming in the designated direction for up to 3s. This move can be manually cancelled, or ended by colliding with a wall. This move will pierce through wild Pokémon, but stops when colliding with Pokémon from the opposing team, dealing damage and stunning or throwing them depending on how far the user travelled.

There are 7 damage thresholds based on how far the user travelled, each one increasing the damage of the point blank hit by 10%. At point blank range the move will stun opposing Pokémon for 0.2s. Travelling a short distance will cause the move to throw opposing Pokémon for 0.45s. Travelling a medium distance will cause the move to throw opposing Pokémon for 0.67s. Travelling a long distance will cause the move to throw opposing Pokémon for 1s.

Upgrade (Level 13): The point blank hit will now throw opposing Pokémon for 0.45s.

moves.name: Big Jaw Bite
moves.description: Has the user let out an intimidating growl, reducing move cooldowns for a short time. When this Unite Move is used again, the user charges at the designated panel from the opposing team and sinks its teeth into them, dealing damage. The amount of damage dealt to Pokémon from the opposing team will be based on the number of additional effects applied by Crunch.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user becomes Unstoppable while letting out an intimidating growl, reducing move cooldowns by 40% of the remaining cooldown, and reduces move cooldowns by 40% of the max cooldown for future move uses while this move is active for up to 15s. Additionally the user's tenacity is increased by 30% for 15s.

When this move is used again, the user becomes Unstoppable and charges at the designated Pokémon from the opposing team and sinks their teeth into them, dealing damage. The amount of damage dealt is based on the number of Fang marks applied by Crunch.

passiveAbility.name: Torrent
passiveAbility.description: While this Pokémon's HP is at half or less; reduces move cooldowns by 20% of the max cooldown for future move uses.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: While this Pokémon's HP is at half or less; reduces move cooldowns by 20% of the max cooldown for future move uses.

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every fifth attack, dealing increased damage and restoring the user's HP. Every time a basic attack or move hits an enemy, basic attack speed is increased.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Gains 1 stack toward boosted attacks after hitting an auto attack or move, stacking up to 5 times. At 5 stacks, all basic attacks become boosted attacks with increased attack speed and additional 30% lifesteal, dealing increased damage to opposing Pokémon, and dealing an additional 10% remaining HP damage to them. All stacks decay after 4s of not hitting an auto attack or move.

Against wild Pokémon: basic attack damage is capped at 1000; boosted attack damage is capped at 1100; additional remaining HP damage is capped at 350.

moves.name: Sand Attack
moves.description: Hurls sand, narrowing the field of vision of enemies and decreases their movement speed for a short time. Also increases movement speed and basic attack speed.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user hurls sand in the designated direction, reducing the field of vision of opposing Pokémon by 50% and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 2s; increasing the user's movement speed by 30% for 2s and auto attack speed by 30% for 3s when this move hits.

moves.name: Dig
moves.description:
Burrow underground and move quickly in the designated direction, jumping out when coming in contact with an enemy or when traveling the maximum distance. This jump deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and throws them.

Upgrade (Level 11): Damage received is reduced for a short time after jumping out of the ground.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user digs underground, becoming untargetable while underground, and dashes quickly forward. The user then jumps out from underground beneath the first opposing Pokémon hit, after traveling max distance, or when coming into contact with an unpassable wall, increasing the user's Defense and Sp. Def for 4s, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon and throwing them for 0.6s.

Upgrade (Level 11): Strengthens the duration of the throw to 1s.

moves.name: Dragon Rush
moves.description:
Back up to create distance before diving to attack in the designated direction, shoving enemies. The user can use Dragon Claw while creating distance at the start of this move to create a combo attack. (This move can crit)

Upgrade (Level 11): Decreases the movement speed of enemies for a short time when they are shoved the maximum distance.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user jumps back and briefly channels, reducing damage received by 45% for 0.9s, and dives forward, dragging all enemies within a small radius to the designated location, then damaging nearby opposing Pokémon afterward.

The user can use Dragon Claw during the first half of the channel to add the Dragon Claw hits as a combo attack. This combo attack scoops up opposing Pokémon, dealing damage with both Dragon Rush and Dragon Claw's first attack and stunning them. If the combo attack hits, the user then throws opposing Pokémon back down with the second Dragon Claw attack, dealing damage with a strengthened slow.

Upgrade (Level 11): Becomes unstoppable during this move; this includes the combo attack with Dragon Claw.

moves.name: Bulldoze
moves.description: Stomp down on the ground, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user stomps down on the ground, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect.

moves.name: Earthquake
moves.description:
Jump high into the air and crash forcefully to the ground, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect.

Upgrade (Level 13): Decrease the movement speed of enemies for a short time if they are in this move's area of effect.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user jumps up and crashes forcefully to the ground, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 2s. Two aftershocks follow the initial crash, dealing less damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect.

Upgrade (Level 13): Strengthens the movement speed decrease of opposing Pokémon near the center of the crash to 40%.

moves.name: Dragon Claw
moves.description:
Attack twice in front of itself using its claws. The first attack deals damage to enemies and throws them. The second attack deals increased damage. After the move ends, basic attack speed is increased for a short time. (This move can crit)

Upgrade (Level 13): Increase damage dealt.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user charges in the designated direction viciously and attacks with two claw swipes. The first attack damages opposing Pokémon and shoves them in the designated direction. The second attack deals more damage to opposing Pokémon and decreases their movement speed by 45% for 2s.

When this move is used in a combo attack with Dragon Rush, the movement speed decrease on the second attack is strengthened to 60% for 2s.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the damage of both attacks.

moves.name: Livid Outrage
moves.description: Has the user unleash a powerful chain attack while dashing forward. The final attack throws enemies but also leaves the user unable to act for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user goes berserk, becoming unstoppable and receiving 30% reduced damage for 5s while unleashing five powerful attacks in a row. The user dashes forward and damages opposing Pokémon in a unique designated area with each attack, taking a brief moment after each attack to aim the next dash. Every attack more damage than the last. The fifth attack throws opposing Pokémon hit and decreases their movement speed by 30% for 1s. The user is left confused for 0.5s afterward.

passiveAbility.name: Rough Skin
passiveAbility.description: When struck by a melee attack, a portion of the damage received is reflected back at the attacker.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: When damaged by a nearby opposing Melee Pokémon, reflects 12.5% of the damage received back to that opposing Pokémon as Attack-based damage (0.6s CD).

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack. When this boosted attack hits, it deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their Sp. Defense by 10% for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and decreasing their Sp. Def by 10% for 2s.

moves.name: Confusion
moves.description: Attacks straight in the designated direction.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user fires a psychic bolt in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and decreasing their movement speed by 35% for 1s.

moves.name: Psychic
moves.description:
After hitting an enemy or traveling its maximum distance, deals damage over time to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their movement speed and Sp. Defense by 20%, stacking up to 3 times.

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduced cooldown.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user launches a psychic bolt in the designated direction which blasts on the first opposing Pokémon hit or after traveling max distance, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. The blast creates a lingering field that slowly moves forward, dealing damage over time to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed by 60% for 1s (up to 5 hits). Each time this move hits opposing Pokémon, their Sp. Def is decreased by 27% for 4s (maximum 81%).

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

moves.name: Moonblast
moves.description:
Has the user attack while moving back to create distance. When the move's blast hits, it deals damage to enemies in a cone and leaves the enemy closest to the user unable to act for a short time.

Upgrade (Level 13): Also grants a shield when this move is used.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user dashes back slightly and launches a blast of moonlight in the designated direction which bursts into a cone behind the first opposing Pokémon hit, stunning that opposing Pokémon for 1.6s and damaging all opposing Pokémon in the cone area of effect, including the target.

Upgrade (Level 13): Grants the user a shield for 3s when launching the blast.

moves.name: Teleport
moves.description: Has the user warp to the designated area, increasing the damage dealt by one basic attack after warping.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user readies a boosted attack and warps forward to the designated location.

When this move is learned, passively increases the damage of Synchronize, auto attacks, and Confusion by 15% until this move is replaced.

moves.name: Psyshock
moves.description:
Attacks the designated location three times. This move's cooldown is reduced each time one of these attacks hits an enemy.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increase the damage this move deals each time one of its attacks hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user channels at a designated area relative to their position before launching three attacks in succession, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and reducing the user's moves' cooldowns by 15% with each attack that hits.

The user is able to attack and move freely while channeling this move.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the damage of the next attack to hit each time an attack hits an opposing Pokémon.

moves.name: Future Sight
moves.description:
Sets a delayed explosion. When the explosion hits an enemy, it reduces this move's cooldown.

Upgrade (Level 11): Reset this move's cooldown when the explosion hits an enemy.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user sets a delayed explosion at the designated area, damaging opposing Pokémon when the delayed explosion hits. If this move hits a Pokémon from the opposing team, the cooldown of this move is reduced by 75% and the user's movement speed is increased by 40% for 1.5s.

Upgrade (Level 11): Resets this move's cooldown when it hits a Pokémon from the opposing team.

moves.name: Fairy Singularity
moves.description: Warps Space in the designated area, drawing in enemies to the center. Shoves and deals damage to enemies the instant the warped space disappears.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user warps space in the designated area, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and pulling them toward the center 4 times. As the warped space disappears, it deals increased damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and shoves them away from the center.

passiveAbility.name: Synchronize
passiveAbility.description: When an enemy inflicts decreased movement speed or a damge-over-time condition on the Pokémon, the same is inflicted on that opponent. This ability goes on cooldown after it's triggered.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: When an opposing Pokémon decreases this Pokémon's movement speed, also decreases that opposing Pokémon's movement speed by 40% for 3s; or, when an opposing Pokémon inflicts a damage-over-time status condition on this Pokémon, also inflicts a damage-over time effect on that opposing Pokémon for 3s (8s CD).

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, lunging at an enemy and dealing damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, lunging at an opposing Pokémon and dealing increased damage to opposing Pokémon hit.

moves.name: Will-o-Wisp
moves.description: Shoot multiple sinister flames, dealing damage to enemies and leaving them burned when it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user shoots 3 sinister flames in a cone in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and burning them for 4s. The first flame deals full damage to each target hit; subsequent flames deal 50% of the full damage to the same target.

The burn deals damage over time every 0.5s for 2s (dealing damage 3 times), and decreases Attack and Sp. Atk by 10% for 4s.

moves.name: Shadow Ball
moves.description:
Hurl a shadowy blob at the target, decreasing the movement speed and Sp. Defense of enemies for a short time when it hits.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increased damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user hurls a shadowy blob in the designated direction which stops at the first Pokémon from the opposing team hit, damaging opposing Pokémon hit, decreasing their movement speed by 90% for 1s, and decreasing their Sp. Def for 3s.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the damage of this move.

moves.name: Sludge Bomb
moves.description:
Hurl unsanitary sludge to the designated area, leaving enemies poisoned when it hits.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increased poison duration.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user hurls unsanitary sludge to the designated area, poisoning opposing Pokémon in the path of the hurl or in the designated area for 5s (dealing 1% max HP Sp. Atk-based damage every 0.5s; maximum 10%), and damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect where the sludge lands.

1% Enemy Max HP every 0.5s (as special damage)

Against wild Pokémon: poison damage is capped at 120 per tick.

Upgrade (Level 13): Strengthens poison duration to 6s (dealing maximum 12% max HP Sp. Atk-based damage).

moves.name: Lick
moves.description: Attacks with the user's tongue and pulls enemies toward the user.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user extends their tongue in the designated direction, stopping at the first opposing Pokémon hit, damaging the target and pulling them toward the user's location.

The user readies a boosted attack when this move is used.

moves.name: Dream Eater
moves.description:
Puts enemies to sleep when it hits. If used again while a nearby enemy is asleep, the user appears behind the target and attacks, restoring HP and reducing the cooldown of Shadow Ball or Sludge Bomb.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increased damage and HP restored.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user projects a hypnotic orb in the designated direction, putting the first opposing Pokémon hit to sleep for 1s and applying a Dream Eater marker on them for 5s. If a nearby opposing Pokémon has a Dream Eater marker or else is asleep when the hypnotic orb disappears, this move can be used again within 5s. This second use has the user instantly teleport behind the target, damaging the target, restoring the user's HP, and reducing the cooldown of Will-O-Wisp, Shadow Ball, or Sludge Bomb by 2s.

The user readies a boosted attack each time this move is used. If the user knocks out a Pokémon from the opposing team, the cooldown of this move is reset.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the damage dealt and HP restored by this move.

moves.name: Hex
moves.description:
Disappear and move to the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect when the user reappears. If this hits an enemy that is afflicted by a status condition, it deals increased damage and its cooldown is reduced.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increased damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user becomes invincible and vanishes, then reappears at the designated location, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. If this move hits an opposing Pokémon afflicted by a status condition, the damage dealt to opposing Pokémon is doubled and the cooldown of this move is reduced by 90%.

The user readies a boosted attack when this move is used.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the damage of this move.

moves.name: Phantom Ambush
moves.description: Jump to the designated area and start sneaking with increased movement speed. If this move is used again, it deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their movement speed for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user becomes unstoppable as they jump forward to the designated location, then enters stealth and increases their movement speed by 30% for 7s. The user exits stealth when they use an attack. If this Unite Move is used again, the user becomes invincible as they jump out to attack, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed by 50% for 1.5s when it hits.

The user readies a boosted attack each time this move is used.

passiveAbility.name: Levitate
passiveAbility.description: Increases movement speed by 10% when out of combat. (5s after leaving combat)
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: When this Pokémon has not been in combat for 5s, their movement speed is increased by 20%.

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage and giving Glaceon 2 ice crystals when the boosted attack hits. Ice crystals last 5s and the duration is refreshed upon gaining additional crystals. Glaceon can hold a maximum of 8 ice crystals at a time.

moves.name: Swift
moves.description: Creates 4 stars around the user. For a short time afterward, each time the user hits an enemy with a basic attack, one of the stars (or 2 stars in the case of a boosted attack) will shoot out and deal additional damage to that enemy.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Creates 4 stars around the user. For a short time afterward, each time the user hits an enemy with a basic attack or boosted attack, one or two of the stars respectively will shoot out and deal additional damage to the target.

moves.name: Icicle Spear
moves.description:
Has the user launch all of their stored ice crystals in rapid succession at the designated enemy. If no ice crystals are stored when this move is used, 2 ice crystals are instantly created and all crystals are launched. The crystals shatter when they hit the designated target, dealing damage to enemies in the area-of-effect. Launched ice crystals can hit enemies on the opposing team that are not the designated target of this move. If this occurs, the ice crystals will still shatter on impact and deal damage in an area in the same way. Ice crystals launched by this move deal increasing damage each time they hit the same enemy. After this move is used, the cooldown of Ice Shard or Freeze-Dry is reduced.

Upgrade (Level 10): Each launched ice crystal also deals additional damage to enemies hit, equal to a set percentage of their max HP.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Glaceon launches all of its stored ice crystals in rapid succession at the designated enemy. If Glaceon has no ice crystals when this move is used, 2 ice crystals are instantly created and are launched. Launched ice crystals can hit enemies on the opposing team that are not the designated target of this move. The crystals shatter when they hit any enemy, dealing damage to all enemies in the area. Ice crystals launched by this move deal  5% increased damage each time they hit the same enemy (capped at 35%). When this move is used, Ice Shard's cooldown is reduced by 2s and Freeze Dry's cooldown is reduced by 2.5s. A maximum of 12 ice crystals may be launched.

Upgrade (Level 10): Each launched ice crystal also deals additional damage to enemies hit equal to 0.5% of their remaining HP.

moves.name: Icy Wind
moves.description:
Jump a short distance in the designated direction. Glaceon's next basic attack launches all stored ice crystals at an enemy, dealing damage to them. Glaceon then regains the same number of ice crystals that were launched. If Glaceon hits the same enemy with 1 or more ice crystals, that enemies movement speed is decreased for a short time. The more ice crystals that hit that target, the stronger this movement speed-decrease effect. If Glaceon has no ice crystals when this move is used, gain 2 ice crystals.

Upgrade (Level 10): Each launched ice crystal also deals additional damage to the enemy hit, equal to a set percentage of their max HP.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Jump a short distance in the designated direction; Glaceon's next auto attack within 5s gains increased range and launches all stored ice crystals at an enemy, dealing damage to them. Glaceon then regains the same number of ice crystals that were launched. If an enemy is hit by 4 or more ice crystals, their movement speed is decreased by 8% per shard (stacking up to 6 times) for 1s. If Glaceon has no ice crystals when this move is used, gain 2 ice crystals.

Upgrade (Level 10): Each launched ice crystal also deals additional damage to enemies hit equal to 1.5% of their remaining HP.

moves.name: Tail Whip
moves.description: The user wags their tail at an enemy, dealing damage and decreasing their Defense, Sp. Def, and movement speed for a short time. The user's next basic attack after using this move becomes a boosted attack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Has the user wag its tail at an enemy, dealing damage and decreasing their Defense and Sp. Def by 30% for 2s, and movement speed by 20% for 1.5s. The next auto attack after using this move becomes a boosted attack.

moves.name: Ice Shard
moves.description:
Increase movement speed for a short time. Afterward, Glaceon's basic attack speed increases for a short time, and basic attacks change into attacks that hurl chunks of ice. These chunks of ice deal damage to enemies hit and decrease their movement speed for a short time. Every time a chunk of ice hits, the user gains 1 ice crystal. The user gains 2 ice crystals if this move is used while launching ice crystals with Icicle Spear, or if it's used after Icy Wind has been used, but before the user has launched all ice crystals with the next basic attack.

Upgrade (Level 12): Also increases basic attack speed for the duration of this move's effect.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Glaceon gains an increase in movement speed by 40% for 1s while basic attack speed is increased by 60% for 2.5s. Basic attacks change into attacks that hurl chunks of ice for 2.5s. These chunks of ice deal damage to enemies they hit and decrease their movement speed by 30% for 1s. Each time a chunk of ice hits, Glaceon gains 2 ice crystals. If this move is used while launching crystals with Icicle Spear, or if this move is used after Icy Wind but before launching all crystals with the next auto attack, gain 2 ice crystals and increase Glaceon's movement speed by 50% for 0.5s.

Upgrade (Level 12): Increases basic attack speed further to 100% for 2.5s.

moves.name: Freeze Dry
moves.description:
Glaceon shoots out a freezing burst of air in a forward direction, increasing Sp. Atk for a short time. . This burst explodes upon hitting an enemy or after traveling a set distance; dealing damage to enemies in the area-of-effect. For each enemy hit by the explosion, Glaceon gains 1 ice crystal. The explosion also leaves behind an ice zone. Enemies that enter this ice zone will have their movement speed decreased. If they remain in the ice zone for a set amount of time, they become frozen for a short time.

Upgrade (Level 12): For each enemy hit by this move's explosions; gain 1 ice crystal.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Shoot out a freezing burst of air in a forward direction. This burst explodes upon hitting an enemy or after traveling a set distance; dealing damage to all enemies in the area. Upon hitting an enemy, increase Glaceon's Sp. Atk by 50% for 4s. For each enemy hit by the explosion, Glaceon gains 2 ice crystals. The explosion also leaves behind an ice zone which slows enemies that enter by 30% for 3s. If they remain in the ice zone for 2.5s, they become frozen for 0.6s.

Upgrade (Level 12): For each enemy hit by this move's explosions, Glaceon gains 1 additional ice crystal.

moves.name: Glacial Stage
moves.description: Leap up high, creating an ice zone in front of Glaceon that deals damage to enemies in the area-of-effect. While Glaceon is in the ice zone, movement speed is increased and Glaceon gains 1 ice crystal whenever a set amount of time passes. The movement speed of enemies in the ice zone is decreased.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Glaceon leaps up high, creating an ice zone in front of themself that deals damage to enemies in the area-of-effect. While Glaceon is in the ice zone, their movement speed is increased by 50% for 6s, and Glaceon gains 1 ice crystals every second while in the zone. The movement speed of enemies in the ice zone is decreased by 50% for 2s.

passiveAbility.name: Run Away
passiveAbility.description: When Eevee would be affected by a hindrance, the hindrance is negated and becomes invincible for 1s. This Ability goes on cooldown after it’s triggered (70s cd).

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage. Hitting a Pokémon afflicted with a Gooey effect with a basic attack reduces all of this Pokémon's move cooldowns by 1.5s.

Boosted attacks spew slime and if the slime hits Pokémon from a distance, a Gooey debuff is applied to those Pokémon and slows by 50% for 0.5s. If the boosted attack is used on a Pokémon in regular attack melee range, the attack deals increased damage.

moves.name: Bubble
moves.description: Has the user cover itself in a bubble, granting it a shield for a short time. When the shield's effect duration ends or if this move is used again, the bubble shield pops, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon and decreasing their movement speed.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Cover itself in a bubble, granting it a shield for 3s. When the shield’s effect duration ends or if this move is used again, the bubble shield pops, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon and slowing them by 30% for 2s.

15% Max HP

moves.name: Muddy Water
moves.description: Has the user splash muddy water around itself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and applying an effect to them that decreases the amount of damage they deal for a short time. If the user deals damage to opposing Pokémon with this move, the user's Defense and Sp. Def are increased for a short time. This effect can stack up to four times.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Splashes muddy water around itself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and applying an effect to them that decreases the amount of damage they deal by 20% for 3s. If the user deals damage to opposing Pokémon with this move, the user’s Defense and Sp. Def are increased for 2s. If Muddy Water is used within 6 seconds, the defense bonus effect is increased in intensity. The defenses bonus effect can stack up to four times.

Upgrade (Level 11): Further increases the user’s Defense and Sp. Def when this move deals damage to opposing Pokémon.

moves.name: Dragon Pulse
moves.description: Has the user unleash a shock wave in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. If the center of the shock wave hits opposing Pokémon, it deals increased damage to them and restores the user's HP. HP is also restored when this move deals damage to opposing Pokémon that have a Gooey slowing effect applied to them.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Unleashes a shock wave in the designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon it hits up to 4 times. If the center of the shock wave hits enemy Pokémon, it deals increased damage to them and restores the user’s HP. More HP is restored when the center of the pulse deals damage to enemy Pokémon that have a Gooey slowing effect applied to them.

10% Missing HP

11% Missing HP

The fourth tick of damage can be animation cancelled by inputs (attack or movement). It is undetermined if this is a bug or not.

Upgrade (Level 11): Restores more HP if the center of the shock wave hits Pokémon, and even more on Pokémon that have a Gooey debuff applied to them.

moves.name: Tackle
moves.description: Has the user charge at an opposing Pokémon, dealing damage and throwing it. If this move hits, the user bounces back a short distance.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Charges at a Pokémon, dealing damage and throwing it. If this move hits, the user bounces back a short distance.

moves.name: Power Whip
moves.description: Has the user lash out with its horns in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed. If this move hits distant opposing Pokémon, those Pokémon are pulled toward the user.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Lashes out with its horns in the designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon in the area of effect and slowing them by 80% for 3s, decaying by 15% every 0.5s. If this move hits distant Pokémon, those Pokémon are pulled toward Goodra.

Upgrade (Level 13): Also applies a Gooey effect when this move hits.

moves.name: Acid Spray
moves.description: Has the user spit acidic fluid at an opposing Pokémon, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in an area of effect centered on the Pokémon it hits and decreasing their movement speed. If this move is used again, the user charges in the designated direction and deals damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. If this charge hits an opposing Pokémon that has been marked by acidic fluid, that Pokémon is thrown, and damage is also dealt to nearby opposing Pokémon.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Spits acidic fluid at target Pokémon, dealing damage to Pokémon in an area of effect centered on the target slowing them by 80% for 3s, decaying by 15% every 0.5s. If this move is used again within 5s, Goodra charges in the designated direction and deals damage to opposing Pokémon hit. If this charge hits a Pokémon that has been marked by acidic fluid, that Pokémon is thrown for 0.75s, and damage is also dealt to nearby opposing Pokémon.

Upgrade (Level 13): Also applies a Gooey effect to opposing Pokémon that receive damage from either the acidic fluid or the charge.

moves.name: Right as Rain
moves.description: Causes rain to fall around the user, gradually restoring the user's HP. While this move is active, all of the user's basic attacks become boosted attacks.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Causes rain to fall around the user, gradually restoring the user’s HP over 6s. While this move is active, all of the user’s basic attacks become boosted attacks.

passiveAbility.name: Gooey
passiveAbility.description: When this Pokémon is hit by an attack, there is a chance it will spread slime around itself. The slime deals damage to Pokémon, Decreases their attack speed by 50% for 2s and applies a Gooey debuff. The Gooey debuff slows Pokémon by 10% for 6s and can stack up to 4 times per Pokémon. Gooey is a debuff that is applicable to Wild Pokémon and enemy players only.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
When this Pokémon is hit by an attack, there is a chance it will spread slime around itself. The slime deals damage to Pokémon, Decreases their attack speed by 50% for 2s and applies a Gooey debuff. The Gooey debuff slows Pokémon by 10% for 6s and can stack up to 4 times per Pokémon. Gooey is a debuff that is applicable to Wild Pokémon and enemy players only.

Additional 1.2% Missing HP per tick

When this Pokémon enters tall grass, it continually recovers HP for up to 3s, until it leaves the tall grass, uses a move, or attacks. When this Pokémon recovers HP due to Gooey, its attack gauge is progressed towards a boosted attack. This HP recovery goes on a 10s cooldown after being triggered.

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage when it hits and causing one berry to drop from Greedent's tail.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage when it hits and causing one Oran berry to drop from Greedent's tail.

moves.name: Tackle
moves.description: Leap to the designated area, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decrease their movement speed for a short time. If this move is used while Covet is active, no area can be designated for the user to leap to. Instead, while the user is running, damage is dealt in an area to enemies in front of Greedent. If Greedent has any berries stashed, consume all of them, reducing Tackle's cooldown for each berry consumed.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Leap to the designated area, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 1s. If this move is used while Covet is active, no area can be designated for Greedent to leap to. Instead, while running, damage is dealt in an area to enemies in front of Greedent. If Greedent has any Oran berries stashed, consume all of them, reducing Tackle's cooldown by 1s for each berry consumed.

moves.name: Bullet Seed
moves.description:
Spit berry seeds in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decrease their movement speed for a short time. The more berries Greedent has stashed, the more damage this move deals.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increased damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Spit berry seeds in the designated direction for 6s; dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 1s. Greedent fires more seeds for each Oran berry stashed.

Fires 1 wave of seeds every 0.8s while held down (up to 7.5 waves). Launches 1 seed every 0.1s per wave; fires a minimum of 3 seeds, plus 1 additional seed per Berry stored before a Berry is consumed (up to 5 additional seeds; maximum 8 seeds total per wave).

Upgrade (Level 13): Increased damage.

moves.name: Belch
moves.description:
Deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and decrease their movement speed for a short time. If Greedent has any berries stashed, consume all of them, reducing Belch's cooldown for each berry consumed.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increased damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and decrease their movement speed by 40% for 2s, decreasing by 10% every 0.5s. If Greedent has any Oran berries stashed, consume all of them, reducing Belch's cooldown by 1s for each berry consumed (capping at 4s). This move's cooldown cannot be reduced beyond 1s.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increased damage.

moves.name: Defense Curl
moves.description: Grants Greedent a shield for a short time. In addition, a berry will drop in front of Greedent.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Grants Greedent a shield (10% max HP) for 3s. In addition, an Oran berry will drop in front of Greedent.

moves.name: Stuff Cheeks
moves.description:
Greedent drops several berries, which can be eaten to recover HP. Any HP recovered that exceeds Greedent's max HP will be converted to a shield. If Greedent catches sight of a berry while using this move, movement speed is increased for a short time.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increase the number of berries dropped.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Greedent drops 5 Oran berries, which can be eaten to recover HP.
