Any HP recovered from an Oran berry eaten within 5s that exceeds Greedent's max HP will be converted to a shield for 1.8% max HP per berry (capped at 9% max HP or 5 berries). If Greedent catches sight of a berry while using this move, movement speed is increased by 70% for 6s, but decreasing by 15% every 1s.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increase the number of Oran berries dropped by 1.

moves.name: Covet
moves.description:
Greedent runs with all its might, becoming unstoppable and dealing damage to enemies hit and throwing them. While running, Greedent drops berries. Eating a dropped berry increases movement speed for a short time. The more berries eaten, the more movement speed is increased. In addition, Belch or Bullet Seed can also be used while running.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increased damage dealt and increased frequency of berries dropping.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Greedent runs with all its might for 6s; becoming resistant to hindrances (for 6.1s), dealing damage to enemies hit and throwing them. While running; Greedent drops Oran berries every 1s. Eating a dropped berry increases movement speed by 22% for 1s (stacking up to 5 times). In addition, Tackle, Belch or Bullet Seed can also be used while running and using this move will reset the cooldown of Tackle or Belch.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increased damage and increased frequency of Oran berries dropping (8 berries over 6s).

moves.name: Berry Belly Flop
moves.description: Greedent eats a berry to recover HP and then leaps to the designated area, dealing damage and throwing enemies within the area of effect. Greedent then eats a special berry, allowing an unlimited amount of berries to be stored for a short time and resets cooldowns of all moves.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Greedent eats a special berry to recover 15% max HP and then leaps to the designated area; dealing damage and throwing enemies within the area of effect for 1s. Greedent then eats another berry, allowing an unlimited amount of Oran berries to be stored for 8s and resets cooldowns of all moves. Greedent is unstoppable during this move.

passiveAbility.name: Cheek Pouch
passiveAbility.description: When Greedent eats a berry, recover HP after receiving the effects of the berry. At the same time, Greedent stashes another berry in its tail. Being hit by a single attack or combination of attacks will cause a berry to drop from Greedent's tail.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
When Greedent eats any berry, recover HP after receiving the effects of the berry. At the same time, Greedent stashes an Oran berry in its tail. Being hit by a single attack or combination of attacks will cause an Oran berry to drop from Greedent's tail (4.5s cd). Berries stay on the ground for 10s.

45 + 5 x (Level - 1) + 2% max HP

23 + 3 x (Level - 1) + 1% Max HP

Oran berry or unite move berry heals Greedent for 1% Max HP + 3x(Lv-1) + 23; Sitrus or Salac berry heals Greedent for 1% Max HP + 0x(Lv-1) + 23. All other players heal from picking up an Oran berry for 2% Max HP + 5x(Level-1) + 45 (level and Max HP scaling is based on the player that ate the Oran berry).

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack. At long range, a boosted attack deals increased damage to enemies and decreases their movement speed for a short time. At close range, a boosted attack deals more damage the lower the enemies remaining HP is.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Auto attacks differ depending on range, and become boosted attacks with every third attack, dealing increased damage. Auto attacks used at long range reduce the cooldown of Bubble, Water Shuriken, or Surf by 0.5s when they hit. Boosted attacks used at long range also decrease the movement speed of the opposing Pokémon hit by 25% for 0.5s.

Auto attacks used in close range instead deal a bonus 5% missing HP damage to opposing Pokémon hit. Boosted attacks used in close range dash through the target, dealing a bonus 10% missing HP damage to opposing Pokémon hit instead. Against wild Pokémon: the total damage of auto attacks used in close range is capped at 500.

moves.name: Bubble
moves.description: Attack with a spray of bubbles, decreasing the movement speed of enemies when it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Sprays 2 bubbles in the designated direction which pop when they hit an opposing Pokémon, damaging opposing Pokémon and decreasing their movement speed by 25% for 1s when each bubble hits.

moves.name: Water Shuriken
moves.description:
Throw shurikens made of water in quick succession. Decreases the movement speed of enemies for a short time and restores the user's HP when it hits. Also increases the user's movement speed while using this move.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the number of shurikens thrown.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Increases movement speed by 25% for 3s and throws 4 shurikens made of water in the designated direction which explode when they hit an opposing Pokémon, damaging nearby opposing Pokémon and decreasing their movement speed by 35% for 1s when each shuriken hits; and restoring HP for each opposing Pokémon hit.

Upgrade (Level 11): Throws 5 shurikens.

moves.name: Surf
moves.description:
Ride a wave, dealing damage to enemies and restoring the user's HP. If this move knocks out an enemy, its cooldown is reset. In addition, this move deals increased damage to enemies with low HP.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the amount of HP restored.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Dashes on a wave forward, damaging opposing Pokémon in the path and restoring HP for each target hit. Deals bonus damage to opposing Pokémon below 50% max HP. If this move hits and the user knocks out an opposing Pokémon before it goes on cooldown, the cooldown of this move is reset.

Against wild Pokémon: the damage of the wave is capped at 1200; bonus damage is capped at 600.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the HP restored by this move.

moves.name: Substitute
moves.description: Leaves behind a substitute doll for the user while it's moving forward.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes invincible and dashes forward, leaving behind a Substitute doll in their place which lasts for 3s.

The Substitute doll has the same HP, Defense and Sp. Def as the user; the Substitute doll receives 500% of the damage the user would take.

moves.name: Double Team
moves.description:
Quickly moves the user away from the current position, creating illusory copies of it to aid in battle.

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduced cooldown.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Briefly vanishes then becomes unstoppable and dashes forward, sending two illusionary copies diagonally behind which last for 3s.

The copies have the same HP, Defense and Sp. Def as Greninja when created, but receive 500% of the damage they would otherwise take. The clones attack nearby opposing Pokémon with Bubble, Water Shuriken, or Surf (whichever move the user has learned), and auto attacks; but deal 15% of the damage they would otherwise deal.

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

moves.name: Smokescreen
moves.description:
Creates a smokescreen and has the user roll away from its current location. Immediately afterward, the user begins sneaking and has its movement speed increased for a short time. This also makes its next basic attack a boosted attack.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increased attack for a short time when the user stops sneaking.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes unstoppable and dashes forward, increasing their movement speed by 30% for 2s, entering stealth for 2s, readying a boosted attack that deals increased damage, and leaving behind a cloud of smoke which lasts for 3s. Exits stealth when using an attack.

Opposing Pokémon in the cloud of smoke have their field of vision reduced by 60%.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases Attack by 20% for 2s when exiting stealth.

moves.name: Waterburst Shuriken
moves.description: Throw a giant water shuriken toward the designated area, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed for a short time when this move hits. The water shuriken then immediately explodes, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and throws them. Once this move ends, the user may jump in a designated direction.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes unstoppable and leaps up to throw a giant water shuriken toward the designated circular location, damaging opposing Pokémon it hits on the way to the circular area of effect (from beyond the range of the user's close range auto attacks) and decreasing the movement speed of those hit by 50% for 2s. The user then suddenly appears on top of the water shuriken which immediately bursts, damaging opposing Pokémon in the circular area of effect again and throwing them.

Leaps off the burst water shuriken and becomes vulnerable to hindrances again as they jump down to the ground in the direction of their choosing.

passiveAbility.name: Torrent
passiveAbility.description: While HP is at 50% or less, Attack and movement speed are increased.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: While at 50% max HP or below, increases Attack by 5% and movement speed by 12%.

moves.name: Attack - Magikarp
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Magikarp has no boosted attack.

Magikarp's basic attacks are a set animation length.

moves.name: Flail
moves.description: Has the user flail about, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon. The lower the user's remaining HP, the more damage this move deals.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Flails on the spot, damaging nearby opposing Pokémon; damage strengthens for every 33% max HP the user is missing.

moves.name: Dragon Breath
moves.description: Has the user exhale a mighty gust, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and leaving them paralyzed. If the user hits an opposing Pokémon with this move, the user's basic attacks are boosted for a short time, dealing increased damage with increased basic attack speed. The more opposing Pokémon hit by this move, the greater the basic attack speed increase.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Exhales a mighty gust in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and paralyzing them for 3s when it hits, decreasing their movement speed and basic attack speed by 30%. If this move hits, gains a buff for 5s; increasing the range and damage of auto attacks; and increasing basic attack speed by a minimum of 30%, strengthening by 10% for each additional opposing Pokémon hit (up to 2 times; maximum 50%).

Upgrade (Level 10): Increases the damage dealt by this move and enhanced auto attacks.

moves.name: Aqua Tail
moves.description: Has the user swing its tail continuously for a set amount of time. If this move hits opposing Pokémon three times, this move can be used again. This second stage of the move has the user charge power then sweep its tail to attack a wider area. If the user charges power for a set amount of time or longer before attacking, the move's area of effect is increased and it deals increased damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
For up to 4s while this move is held; moves slightly in the designated direction then swings a blade of water from their tail in the designated direction (up to 5 times), damaging opposing Pokémon hit with each blade.

If at least 3 blades of water hit opposing Pokémon before this move is released or ends, a second stage of this move can be used within 5s; becomes unstoppable and charges for up to 1.5s, then attacks with a large sweep of their tail in a wider area forward, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. If the second stage is charged for at least 0.5s, increases the range of the area of effect and damage dealt.

Upgrade (Level 10): The boosted aqua tail deals an additional 20% missing HP damage to opposing Pokémon. Against wild Pokémon: additional missing HP damage is capped at 1000.

moves.name: Splash
moves.description: Has the user jump to the designated location. A maximum of 3 uses can be kept in reserve for this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Jumps to the designated location forward.

A maximum of 3 uses can be kept in reserve, with a 2s cooldown between uses.

moves.name: Waterfall
moves.description: Has the user charge three times in a row at awesome speed, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. The user can change direction between each charge. If the second charge hits an opposing Pokémon, the user is granted a shield. If the third charge hits an opposing Pokémon, the opposing Pokémon is thrown.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Charges forward up to 3 times, damaging opposing Pokémon hit with each charge; waiting for at least 0.07s before dashing forward with the next charge. The first charge is a regular dash forward; the next movement within 2s after the last charge dashes forward with the same effect. Dragon Breath or Aqua Tail can be used within the 2s duration in between charges without interrupting this move's course. Using Dragon Current ends this move early.

13% Max HP + 260

If two charges hit opposing Pokémon, grants a shield for 2s. If all three charges hit opposing Pokémon, the third charge throws opposing Pokémon hit for 0.45s.

Upgrade (Level 12): Reduce this move's cooldown by 1s.

moves.name: Bounce
moves.description: Has the user charge power, then bounce to the designated location and attack. The longer power is charged, the farther the user can bounce. If power is charged for a certain amount of time or longer, the user is granted a shield when it bounceds, and the user applies a movement speed decrease to opposing Pokémon it hits when it lands.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes unstoppable and charges power for up to 2.4s, becoming fully charged after 0.8s; then bounces to the designated location forward, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area upon impact. While charging power, increases the range of this move, reaching maximum range when fully charged.

If fully charged when released, grants a shield for 5s, and throws opposing Pokémon hit for 0.45s. If this move is canceled, it goes on a 2s cooldown.

Upgrade (Level 12): Strengthens the value of the shield effect.

moves.name: Dragon Current
moves.description: Has the user dive underwater, where it can't attack or be targeted by opposing Pokémon. When this move is used again, the user leaps up forcefully and attacks. If the user stays underwater for a set amount of time, it creates a whirlpool where it surfaces, applying a continuous slowing effect to opposing Pokémon it hits. If the user stays underwater for an even longer amount of time, it creates a waterspout where it surfaces, throwing opposing Pokémon it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes untargetable and dives underwater for up to 4s while this Unite Move is held, increasing movement speed by 45%. When released or after 4s, forcefully leaps up and attacks, damaging opposing Pokémon hit. The longer the move is held, the larger the area of effect becomes.

If the user stays underwater for at least 1s before releasing the Unite Move, strengthens the movement speed increase to 55%; deals increased damage and creates a whirlpool where they surface, dealing damage over time to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect (up to 5 times) and decreasing their movement speed for 4s when hit. If underwater for at least 2s, strengthens the movement speed increase to 65%; deals even more damage and creates a waterspout where they surface instead, throwing opposing Pokémon hit for 1s.

passiveAbility.name: Rattled
passiveAbility.description: When damaged, increases movement speed by 5% for 3s (up to 5 times; maximum 25%).
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
When damaged, increases movement speed by 5% for 3s (up to 5 times; maximum 25%).

Increases the effort gauge each time Magikarp uses an auto attack or move, hits with an auto attack or move, scores a goal, knocks out a Pokémon from the opposing team or assists in knocking out a Pokémon from the opposing team. Once the effort gauge is full, Magikarp evolves into Gyarados.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage.

Magikarp and Mega Evolved Gyarados do not have a boosted attack. Mega Evolved Gyarados lunges when using a basic attack.

moves.name: Flail
moves.description: Has the user flail about, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon. The lower the user's remaining HP, the more damage this move deals.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Flails on the spot, damaging nearby opposing Pokémon; damage strengthens for every 33% max HP the user is missing.

moves.name: Dragon Breath
moves.description: Has the user exhale a mighty gust, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and leaving them paralyzed. If the user hits an opposing Pokémon with this move, the user's basic attacks are boosted for a short time, dealing increased damage with increased basic attack speed. The more opposing Pokémon hit by this move, the greater the basic attack speed increase.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Exhales a mighty gust in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and paralyzing them for 3s when it hits, decreasing their movement speed and basic attack speed by 30%. If this move hits, gains a buff for 5s; increasing the range and damage of auto attacks; and increasing basic attack speed by a minimum of 30%, strengthening by 10% for each additional opposing Pokémon hit (up to 2 times; maximum 50%).

If the user is mega evolved, instead Mega Gyarados becomes unstoppable then chases down and performs 5 special auto attacks on targets hit by this attack.

Upgrade (Level 10): Increases the damage dealt by this move and basic attacks.

moves.name: Splash
moves.description: Has the user jump to the designated location. A maximum of 3 uses can be kept in reserve for this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Jumps to the designated location forward.

A maximum of 3 uses can be kept in reserve, with a 2s cooldown between uses.

moves.name: Waterfall
moves.description: Has the user charge three times in a row at awesome speed, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. The user can change direction between each charge. If the second charge hits an opposing Pokémon, the user is granted a shield. If the third charge hits an opposing Pokémon, the opposing Pokémon is thrown.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Charges forward up to 3 times, damaging opposing Pokémon hit with each charge; waiting for at least 0.07s before dashing forward with the next charge. The first charge is a regular dash forward; the next movement within 2s after the last charge dashes forward with the same effect. Dragon Breath can be used within the 2s duration in between charges without interrupting this move's course. Using Dragon Current ends this move early. This move's cooldown begins when the move ends. If two charges hit opposing Pokémon, grants a shield for 2s. If all three charges hit opposing Pokémon, the third charge throws opposing Pokémon hit for 0.45s.

If the user is mega evolved, instead the cooldown begins when the move is used. Additionally, the previous shield and throw effects are granted even if the move misses, and the cooldown of the move is reduced by 10% for each dash that hits.

Upgrade (Level 12): Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

moves.name: Dragon Current
moves.description: Has the user dive underwater, where it can't attack or be targeted by opposing Pokémon. When this move is used again, the user leaps up forcefully and attacks. If the user stays underwater for a set amount of time, it creates a whirlpool where it surfaces, applying a continuous slowing effect to opposing Pokémon it hits. If the user stays underwater for an even longer amount of time, it creates a waterspout where it surfaces, throwing opposing Pokémon it hits. After using this Unite Move, this Pokémon Mega Evolves.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes untargetable and dives underwater for up to 4s while this Unite Move is held, increasing movement speed by 45%. When the button is released, the user leaps up forcefully and attacks. If the user stays underwater for 1s, Gyarados strengthens the movement speed increase to 55% and creates a whirlpool when surfacing, applying a continuous slow to opposing Pokémon hit. If the user stays underwater for 2s, Gyarados strengthens the movement speed increase to 65% and creates a waterspout when surfacing, throwing opposing Pokémon hit for 1s. The longer the move is held, the larger the area of effect becomes.

After using this Unite Move, the user Mega Evolves for 15 seconds.

passiveAbility.name: Swift Swim
passiveAbility.description: When the Pokémon uses a move, their movement speed increases by 30% for 3s. Each time this Pokémon knocks out an opposing Pokémon, scores a goal, or otherwise makes a contribution in battle, their effort gauge increases. Once the gauge is full, the Pokémon evolves into Gyarados.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: When the Pokémon uses a move, their movement speed increases by 30% for 3s. Each time this Pokémon knocks out an opposing Pokémon, scores a goal, or otherwise makes a contribution in battle, their effort gauge increases. Once the gauge is full, the Pokémon evolves into Gyarados.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, shooting out a sweeping flame that deals increased damage and restores HP per enemy Pokémon hit. While firing the sweeping flame Ho-Oh's movement speed is decreased by 30%.

moves.name: Safeguard
moves.description: Has the user create a protective field that reduces damage taken from the next attack. When this field reduces damage from an attack, the user's movement speed is increased for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Create a protective field around the user that reduces the next incoming source of damage by 50%. When this field reduces damage from an attack, the user's movement speed is increased by 40% for 3s.

moves.name: Sacred Fire
moves.description: Has the user fly higher, allowing it to move freely over walls, and increasing its movement speed and Attack. After using this move, the user's next three basic attacks become boosted attacks. When this move is used again, the user lands on the ground forcefully, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Shoot a mystical fireball at an area, dealing damage to non-allied Pokémon it hits, stunning them for 0.6s, and leaving them burned for 3s, reducing their Attack by 10%. Then fly up high into the air for up to 6s. The next 3 basic attacks are boosted attacks. While flying, the user is able to move freely over walls and its attack is increased by 25% and movement speed is increased by 30%. If this move is used again, the user lands cancelling the attack and movement speed bonuses.

Ho-Oh may not interact with objects on the ground such as Berries or Aeos Energy but may score on goal zones.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the Attack reduction of the burn to 20%.

moves.name: Fire Spin
moves.description: Has the user generate a blazing vortex around itself, dealing damage and applying a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon it hits. The user redirects a portion of damage taken by ally Pokémon inside the vortex to itself. At the start of this move, the user receives a shield effect. This move ends if the user loses this shield. While the user is still shielded, if a set amount of time passes or if this move is used again, the user releases a heat wave around itself that deals damage to opposing Pokémon. The more damage the user takes while the shield effect is active, the more damage this heat wave deals. The emitted heat wave makes opponents unable to act.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Generate a blazing vortex around yourself and gain a shield. While the vortex is active, Ho-Oh slows itself by 40% and cannot attack. The first time an enemy interacts with the vortex, they receive an 80% movement speed decrease decaying by 10% every 0.5s over 4s. The vortex also deals damage to enemies. If allies are within the vortex, 60% of post-mitigation damage those allies would take is redirected to Ho-Oh as true damage (without ignoring shields).

33% Max HP

If there is a shield after 4s or if this move is used again while this move's shield is intact, a heat wave is released around the user, dealing damage to enemies, and additional damage equal to the percentage of the damage the shield received. The emitted heat wave additionally stuns non-allied Pokémon it hits for 0.6s,  There is a 1.5s delay before the shield may be detonated from the start of the vortex. If the shield is destroyed before detonation, no damage is dealt.

Upgrade (Level 13): When the shield is broken, the heat wave is now automatically released.

moves.name: Tailwind
moves.description: Has the user whip up a whirlwind in the designated direction, dealing damage to the opposing Pokémon it hits. The whirlwind then remains on the field for a short time. Ally Pokémon moving in the same direction as the whirlwind have their movement speed increased, while opposing Pokémon moving against the whirlwind have their movement speed decreased.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Whip up a whirlwind in designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit. The whirlwind then remains on the field for 5s. Allies moving in the same direction as the whirlwind have their movement speed increased by 30% as long as they are in the field and lasting for 0.5s after stepping out of the field. Opposing Pokémon moving against the whirlwind have their movement speed decreased by 30% lasting for 0.5s after stepping out of the field.

moves.name: Flamethrower
moves.description: Has the user blow flames in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. This move's cooldown increases the longer the user blows flames. If the user hits Pokémon from the opposing team with this move, the user recovers HP. If the user continues to hit opposing Pokémon with this move, the Pokémon are burned, decreasing their movement speed. When the burned Pokémon moves, it will leave behind scorched ground. Opposing Pokémon that touch this scorched ground will also be burned, decreasing their movement speed.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Blow flames in a designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit every 0.25s and slowing Ho-Oh's movement speed by 15%. This move may be held for up to 4s to blow flames for longer. This move's cooldown increases the longer flames are blown for. If enemies are hit, the user recovers HP. If enemies are hit thrice, they are burned, refreshing on additional hits of flamethrower.

Burned enemies take damage over 3s at 0.5s intervals. Burned enemies also have their movement speed decreased by 15% for 1.5s, their attack decreased by 20% for 2s, and their special attack decreased by 20% for 1.5s. Enemies burned by Flamethrower will leave behind a scorched area. Enemies traversing the scorched area are slowed by 15%. Enemies that touch the area will also receive burns but this burn will not spread the scorched area.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the length of time flames may be blown to 5s.

moves.name: Sky Attack
moves.description: Has the user spread its wings and charge, then return to its original position, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and burning the ground along its flight path for a short time. Burning ground deals damage to opposing Pokémon and applies a slowing effect. When this move is used again, or if another move is used mid-charge, the user stops in place.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Ho-Oh spreads their wings and charges in a loop. While charging, deal damage to enemies hit and burn the ground along the flight path for 4.5s. The burning ground deals damage to enemies that touch it every 0.5s and slows them by 30% for 1.5s. If this move is used again or if another move is used mid-charge, then the user stops in place.

During the flight, Ho-Oh receives 30% less damage.

Upgrade (Level 11): While the Ho-Oh is on burning ground, gradually restore HP.

moves.name: Rekindling Flame
moves.description: When a nearby ally Pokémon is knocked out, a feather is left in its place for a short time. This move has the user spread its wings wide and consume all of the Aeos energy it has to revive knocked-out ally Pokémon, returning them to battle at their respective feathers. The more Aeos energy that's consumed, the more ally Pokémon are revived.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: When a nearby ally is knocked out, a feather is left in their place until they respawn at base. When Rekindling Flame is used, Ho-oh spreads their wings and consumes all of the Aeos energy held to revive knocked-out allies where their feathers are for 60% of their maximum HP. Rekindling Flame may only be used if there are feathers nearby. The number of available revivals are based on how much Aeos energy is consumed. One ally may be always revived. Up to two allies are revivable if at least 9 energy is consumed. Up to three allies are revivable if at least 17 Aeos energy is consumed. Up to four allies are revivable if at least 25 Aeos energy is consumed.

passiveAbility.name: Regenerator
passiveAbility.description: If this Pokémon doesn't take damage from enemy Pokémon (Wild Pokémon do not count) for 3s, it will recover 3% max HP every 2 seconds. Damage to shields will not deactivate Regenerator. Ho-oh additionally recovers 0.3% max HP every 2 seconds.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: If this Pokémon doesn't take damage from enemy Pokémon (Wild Pokémon do not count) for 3s, it will recover 3% max HP every 2 seconds. Damage to shields will not deactivate Regenerator. Ho-oh additionally recovers 0.3% max HP every 2 seconds.

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing damage to enemies by unleashing a beam of light. When Hoopa is unbound, every attack becomes a boosted attack and deals damage to up to 2 additional nearby enemies.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing damage to enemies by unleashing a beam of light.

moves.name: Astonish
moves.description: Deals damage to enemies in the designated area of effect, leaving them unable to act for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Deals damage to enemies in the designated area of effect, leaving them unable to act for .75s.

moves.name: Phantom Force
moves.description:
Hoopa creates a portal ring under itself and warps to a designated location, dealing damage to nearby enemies at that location and shoving them. Hoopa then shoots two sinister flames that deal damage to enemies hit and decreases their movement speed for a short time. This move can be used a second time within a set amount of time. If used a second time, Hoopa can return to the initial location they warped from. When using this move, if there is a nearby ally that is linked by Trick, that ally can also warp to the initial ring's location. The ally that warps in this way will recover HP.

Upgrade (Level 12): Become invincible for a short time when this move is used.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Hoopa creates a portal ring under themself and warps to a designated location, dealing damage to nearby enemies at that location and shoving them. Hoopa then shoots two sinister flames that deal damage to enemies hit and decreases their movement speed by 40% for 2s. This move can be activated a second time within 3.5s and if activated, Hoopa can return to the initial ring's location. If there is a nearby ally that is also linked by Trick, that ally can warp to the initial ring's location and recover 15% max HP.

Upgrade (Level 12): Become invincible for .6s when this move is used.

moves.name: Shadow Ball
moves.description:
Unleash a shadowy blob in front of Hoopa, dealing damage to enemies hit and decreases their movement speed and Sp. Def for a short time. If Hoopa then hits the same enemy with a move or boosted attack within a set amount of time, it deals additional damage and leaves the enemy unable to act for a short time. When using this move, if there is a nearby ally linked by Trick, this move will also unleash a shadowy blob at the same target from that ally's location.

Upgrade (Level 12): The shadowy blob will now explode before disappearing. Enemies hit by the explosion are affected as if they were hit by the shadowy blob itself.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Unleash a shadowy blob in front of Hoopa, dealing damage to enemies hit and decreasing their movement speed by 50% for 1s and Sp. Defense by 30% for 3s. If Hoopa then hits the same enemy with a move or boosted attack within 4s, deal additional damage and leave the enemy unable to act for .8s. When using this move, if there is a nearby ally linked by Trick, this move will also unleash a shadowy blob at the same target from that ally's location.

Upgrade (Level 12): The shadowy blob will now explode before disappearing. Enemies hit by the explosion are affected as if they were hit by the shadowy blob itself.

moves.name: Confusion
moves.description: Unleash a telekinetic force in the designated direction, dealing damage to the first enemy hit. The telekinetic force clings to that enemy and then causes a delayed explosion. The explosion deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their movement speed for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Unleash a telekinetic force in the designated direction, dealing damage to the first enemy hit. The telekinetic force clings to that enemy and then causes a delayed explosion. The explosion deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their movement speed by 50% for 0.5s.

moves.name: Hyperspace Hole
moves.description:
Hoopa creates a large ring at the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect. After a set amount of time, Hoopa and any allies in the area of effect will return to their base, and their movement speed will be increased for a short time. These allies can also warp back to where the ring was created. An intact ally forward goal zone can also be designated as the location where the ring is created. Creating the ring on this forward goal zone of the top path will also create a ring on the forward goal zone of the bottom path, and vice versa. All allies, including Hoopa, can use these rings to warp between the two goal zones, and their movement speed will be increased for a short time after warping. After an ally warps, there is a set cooldown before that ally can use these rings again. If a path's forward goal zone is destroyed, the middle goal zone on the same path will take its place. This move cannot be used on base goal zones.

Upgrade (Level 10): Also increases the movement speed of allies near the ring when they are heading towards it.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Hoopa creates a large ring at the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect. After 3s, Hoopa and any allies in the area of effect will return to their base, and their movement speed will be increased for a short time. These allies can also manually warp back to where the ring was created within 3s. An intact ally forwardmost goal zone can also be designated as the location where the ring is created. Creating a ring on the goal zone of the top path will also create a ring on the forwardmost goal zone of the bottom path for 6.5s, and vice versa. All allies (including Hoopa) can use these rings to manually warp between the two goal zones, and their movement speed will be increased for a short time after warping. After an ally or Hoopa warps, there is a 10s cooldown before that Pokémon can manually warp again. If a path's forwardmost goal zone is destroyed, the middle goal zone on the same path will take its place. This move cannot be used on base goal zones. Hyperspace Hole's portal usage cooldown applies only to Hyperspace Hole teleports, and is reset upon usage of Unite Move. If there are multiple effects that would allow you to teleport (ie Phantom Force and Rings Unbound), Rings Unbound will take priority. Hyperspace Holes can be seen by enemies on the minimap.

Upgrade (Level 10): Also increases movement speed by 70% for allies near the ring when they are heading towards it.

moves.name: Trick
moves.description:
Hoopa creates a link between itself and an ally using a ring, increasing both of their movement speeds and granting them each a shield for a short time. When Hoopa uses Shadow Ball or performs a basic attack, the ring on the linked ally also unleashes the same move or attack. Hoopa can also use this move on themself, increasing movement speed and granting a shield for a short time. Additionally, it increases the damage dealt by Shadow Ball.

Upgrade (Level 10): Also restores the linked ally Pokémon's HP when it deals damage to enemies.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Hoopa creates a link between itself and an ally, increasing both of their movement speeds by 60% for 3s, decaying 10% per second down to 40% increased movement speed, and granting them each a shield for 3s. For the next 7s, when Hoopa uses Shadow Ball or performs an auto attack, the ring on the linked ally also unleashes the same move or attack and if both attacks hit the same target, the second deals 50% damage. Hoopa can also use this move on themself, increasing movement speed and granting a shield for a short time. Additionally, increase the damage dealt by Shadow Ball while Trick is active.

This can trigger multiple times.

Upgrade (Level 10): Hoopa and the linked ally each heal for 10% of their own damage dealt. This effect lasts for 3s.

moves.name: Rings Unbound
moves.description: Transforms Hoopa into Hoopa Unbound for a short time, increasing max HP and creating a ring at the designated location. Each ally can warp to the ring's location one time. As Hoopa Unbound, moves change to Hyperspace Fury and Psybeam. Hoopa Unbound cannot score goals or activate the Magician ability.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Hoopa becomes invincible for 0.6s while transforming into Hoopa Unbound, then becomes unstoppable for 0.3s as it opens a large portal ring at a designated location. Hoopa Unbound lasts for 15s, increasing max HP by 40% and changes Hoopa's regular moves to Hyperspace Fury and Psybeam. Hoopa Unbound cannot score goals or activate the Magician ability.

The portal ring created stays open for 10s, and allies can warp to that location once within the duration of the ring. This portal is indicated on the mini map where all players may see where it is.

passiveAbility.name: Magician
passiveAbility.description: In battles where the map is divided into an ally team side and an opposing team side, this ability creates a hole under one of the berries on the opposing team's side (chosen at random) and warps it in front of Hoopa. If there are no berries, but there is Aeos Energy on the ground, then some energy is warped in front of Hoopa instead. In the event that no berries from the opposing team's side are warped, the cooldown of this ability is reduced.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: In battles where the map is divided into an ally team side and an opposing team side, this ability creates a hole under one of the berries on the opposing team's side (chosen at random) and warps it in front of Hoopa (40s cooldown). If there are no berries, but there is Aeos Energy on the ground, then some energy is warped in front of Hoopa instead. In the event that no berries from the opposing team's side are warped, the cooldown of this ability is reduced to 20s. Berries last 30 seconds and take 2 seconds to appear after magician's activation.

builds.name: Phantom Trick
builds.emblemName: Special Attack Cooldown Reduction - Mobility for Survivability

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage with an increased critical-hit rate.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage with 20% increased critical-hit rate.

moves.name: Tearful Look
moves.description: Decreases the Attack and Sp. Atk of the target Pokémon by 20% for 3s. Inteleon gains a critical counter and increases its movement speed.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Decreases the Attack and Sp. Atk of the target Pokémon by 20% for 3s. Inteleon gains a critical counter and increases its movement speed by 40% for 3s (decreasing over time).

moves.name: Fell Stinger
moves.description: Has the user fire a sharp stinger in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. If the stinger hits a wall, the user moves to the point the stinger hit, and this move's cooldown is reduced. If the stinger hits an opposing Pokémon or a wall, the user gains one critical counter.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Inteleon fires a sharp stinger in the designated direction. The stinger stops at the first Pokémon hit or when it reaches a wall. Inteleon gains two critical counters if either a Pokémon is hit or a wall is hit. If it hits a Pokémon it deals damage. If it hits a wall, this move's cooldown is reduced by half and Inteleon is pulled to the wall and starts camouflaging.

Upgrade (Level 11): Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

moves.name: Acrobatics
moves.description: Has the user glide in the designated direction, and if the user hits an opposing Pokémon or wall, it can glide in a designated direction again. If the user hits an opposing Pokémon while gliding, the user deals damage to it and decreases its movement speed. Every time this move is used, the user gains one critical counter. This move can also be used while attacking with Liquidation.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Inteleon glides in the designated direction. If it hits a Pokémon or a wall, it can glide in a designated direction a second time. If Inteleon hits a Pokémon while gliding, Inteleon deals damage to it and decreases its movement speed by 30% for 2s. Every time Inteleon glides in a designated direction, it gains a critical counter. If Inteleon bounces off a wall or Pokémon, Liquidation's current cooldown is reduced by 40%.

This move can also be used while attacking with Liquidation.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the first glide's travel distance.

moves.name: Water Gun
moves.description: Has the user squirt water, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. This move has a set chance of becoming a critical hit.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Inteleon squirts water, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits.

moves.name: Snipe Shot
moves.description: Has the user aim in the designated direction for a set amount of time and then fire a water projectile, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits while partially ignoring their Sp. Def. The farther away the user is from the opposing Pokémon it hits, the more damage this move does. This move has a set chance of becoming a critical hit. A maximum of three uses can be kept in reserve for this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Inteleon aims for up to 6s and then fires, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits while partially ignoring their Sp. Def. by 15%/20%/25% depending on distance (close, mid, far respectively). Aiming reduces the possible spread of the shot, reaching maximum precision after aiming for 1.25s. The farther away the user is from the Pokémon it hits, the more damage it does. A maximum of three uses can be kept in reserve for this move. There is a 1.5s cooldown between uses.

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduces the cooldown for charges by 1s.

moves.name: Liquidation
moves.description: Has the user continually fire water projectiles from both hands, dealing damage to the designated opposing Pokémon it hits. If this move hits {0} times within a short period of time, damage the opposing Pokémon takes from the user's attacks will be increased for a short time. This move has a set chance of becoming a critical hit. This move can also be used while moving via Acrobatics.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Inteleon starts firing water projectiles at the designated Pokémon. Inteleon fires a total of 8 projectiles in 1.7s and is slowed by 40% while firing. Water projectiles can hit targets other than the designated Pokémon and only deal damage to the first Pokémon they hit. If this move hits the same Pokémon 5 times in a short time, Inteleon will deal 20% increased damage to this Pokémon for 3s.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the number of projectiles shot during the duration to 10.

moves.name: Azure Spy Vision
moves.description: For a short time, enables the user to see nearby Pokémon that are in stealth or hiding in tall grass. Also doubles the user's critical-hit rate, and the user gains one critical counter whenever a set amount of time passes.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
For 10.5s, Inteleon can see nearby Pokémon that are in stealth or hiding in tall grass.

While active, also doubles Inteleon's critical-hit rate, and gives 1 critical counter per second.

passiveAbility.name: Sniper
passiveAbility.description: When this Pokémon stays near a wall for 1s it becomes camouflaged, making opposing Pokémon unable to target it, and starts gaining critical counters (up to a maximum of 3). A critical counter is gained as soon as Inteleon is camouflaged, with a new counter gained every 3s while camouflaged. Hitting any Pokémon prevents Inteleon from becoming camouflaged for 3s. When a critical counter is consumed, Inteleon's next attack or move that can critically strike is guaranteed to be a critical hit.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
When this Pokémon stays near a wall for 1s it becomes camouflaged, making opposing Pokémon unable to target it, and starts gaining critical counters. Sobble may only hold one critical counter. Drizzile may hold two. Inteleon may hold three. While camouflaged, Inteleon is slowed by 10%. A critical counter is gained as soon as Inteleon is camouflaged, with a new counter gained every 3s while camouflaged. Dealing or receiving damage breaks the camouflage and prevents Inteleon from starting to camouflage for 3s. When a critical counter is consumed, Inteleon's next attack or move that can critically strike is guaranteed to be a critical hit.

Critical hits deal 250% rather than 200% damage.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, shooting water through the target and dealing increased damage to Pokémon hit by the spray. The boosted attack also heals Lapras.

moves.name: Whirlpool
moves.description: Has the user create a whirlpool centered on itself that lasts for a short time, dealing damage over time to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. The time between hits is shorter the farther opposing Pokémon are from the whirlpool's center.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Create a whirlpool centered on Lapras that lasts for 3s, dealing damage over time to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. The time between hits is shorter the farther opposing Pokémon are from the whirlpool’s center.

moves.name: Water Pulse
moves.description: Has the user release a pulse of water centered on itself that spreads out, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and decreasing their movement speed. For each opposing Pokémon hit by a pulse from this move, an additional pulse will be released the next time this move is used, up to a total of 2 extra pulses. After the first pulse after activating this move hits an opposing Pokémon, the user's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Release a pulse of water centered on itself that spreads out, dealing damage to Pokémon it hits and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 2s. For each opposing Pokémon hit by a pulse from this move, an additional pulse will be released the next time this move is used within 8 seconds, up to a total of 3 pulses. If the first pulse after activating this move hits a Pokémon, the user’s next basic attack becomes a boosted attack.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the maximum number of pulses to 4.

moves.name: Perish Song
moves.description: Has the user sing an eerie melody that echoes around it, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. This move can be used up to three times consecutively within a set time, and opposing Pokémon hit all three times take additional damage. Opposing Pokémon that take this additional damage will be knocked out if their HP falls below a set percentage of their max HP.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Sing an eerie melody that echoes around yourself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. This move can be used up to three times consecutively within 5s of its last use. Pokémon hit all three times take additional damage equal to one Perish Song hit. If the additional damage would bring an enemy Pokémon below 10% of their max HP, that enemy Pokémon will take 40% of their maximum HP as True Damage.

Upgrade (Level 11): The next basic attack after using this move becomes a boosted attack.

moves.name: Ice Shard
moves.description: Has the user hurl chunks of ice in a forward direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon they hit and leaving them frozen for a short time. If this move hits an opposing Pokémon that is already frozen, it deals damage to it and shoves it.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Hurl 5 chunks of ice in a forward direction, dealing damage to Pokémon they hit and leaving them frozen for 0.6s. If this move hits a Pokémon that is already frozen, it deals damage to it and shoves in the direction of the ice shards for 0.4s.

moves.name: Bubble Beam
moves.description: Grants the user a shield and has the user spray bubbles in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. After the bubbles fly a set distance, they slowly float for a short time. Opposing Pokémon hit by a floating bubble are enveloped by it and left unable to act for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Create a bubble shield on Lapras for up to 4s or until the shield is broken, then spray 5 bubbles with a beam in the designated direction. Pokémon hit by the bubbles take damage and are enveloped by it, leaving them unable to act for 1s. After the bubbles fly a set distance, they slowly float for 3s. Pokémon hit by a floating bubble are enveloped by it and left unable to act for 1s, but will not be damaged.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the strength of the shield granted by this move.

moves.name: Ice Beam
moves.description: Has the user unleash an icy-cold beam of energy in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and freezing the ground. Opposing Pokémon on the frozen ground take damage over time and have their movement speed decreased, and they become frozen if they remain on the frozen ground for a set amount of time. When the user is on the frozen ground, its movement speed is increased.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Unleash an icy-cold beam of energy in the designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon hit and freezing the ground for 3s. Pokémon on the frozen ground take damage equal to the beam's damage every 0.4s they remain on it and have their movement speed decreased by 15% for 2s stacking up to 6 times. Pokémon become frozen for 1s if they remain on the frozen ground for 3 ticks. When Lapras is on the frozen ground, Lapras's movement speed is increased by 20%, lingering for 0.5s if Lapras leaves the frozen ground.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increase the duration Pokémon are Frozen for to 1.5s.

moves.name: Lapras Express
moves.description: Has the user ride a wave quickly and continuously forward. The user's movement speed increases while riding but decreases if the user hits an obstacle. If the user hits an opposing Pokémon, the user stops in place, dealing damage to and throwing the opposing Pokémon. While the user is riding the wave, one nearby ally Pokémon of the user's choice can ride on its back. If this move is used again, the user stops and launches a wave that continues moving forward, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and throwing them. Ally Pokémon riding on the user's back can dismount whenever they choose.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Ride a wave quickly and continuously forward becoming Unstoppable for up to 15s. Lapras’s movement speed increases every second while riding but decreases if the user hits an obstacle. If this move is used again, the user stops and launches a wave that continues moving forward, dealing damage to Pokémon it hits and throwing them for 1.5s. If the user hits an enemy Pokémon while still riding, the user stops in place, dealing damage to and throwing the opposing Pokémon for 1.5s. While the user is riding the wave, one nearby ally Pokémon of the user’s choice within range may be picked up and can ride on its back. Ally Pokémon riding on the user’s back can dismount whenever they choose.

passiveAbility.name: Shell Armor
passiveAbility.description: If Lapras would take a single instance of damage that would be greater than 10% of Lapras's maximum HP, this damage will be reduced by 20%.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: If Lapras would take a single instance of damage that would be greater than 10% of Lapras's maximum HP, this damage will be reduced by 20%.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Shoots a psychic projectile at an opposing Pokémon, damaging them.

Latias has no boosted attack.

moves.name: Confusion
moves.description: Has the user release a weak telekinetic force in the designated direction, dealing damage and applying a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Releases a weak telekinetic force in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 2s.

moves.name: Mist Ball
moves.description: Has the user release a mist-like flurry of down in the designated direction, dealing damage, applying a slowing effect, and marking opposing Pokémon it hitos. After a set amount of time pases, or if the move is used again, the flurry of down bursts, creating a zone of mist. Opposing Pokémon in the zone take damage, receive a slowing effect, and are marked. Marked opposing Pokémon deal decreased damage. If either Latias or Latios deal damage to opposing marked Pokémon, the duration those Pokémon remain marked increases.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Releases a mist-like flurry of down in the designated direction. When used again or after 0.9s pass: the flurry of down bursts, creating a zone of mist in the area of effect dealing damage up to 2 more times. This move then goes on cooldown. The projectile and area of effect damages opposing Pokémon in it, decreases their movement speed by 40% for 2s, and marks them for 2s.

Marked opposing Pokémon deal 25% decreased damage. If either Latias or Latios deals damage to marked opposing Pokémon: increases the duration of the mark by 1s. This duration extension can be continued indefinitely.

Upgrade (Level 12): Increased mark base duration to 3s.

moves.name: Dragon Cheer
moves.description: Has the user rally the designated ally Pokémon, granting its next 2 basic attacks additional damage. The move can be used again to rally a second designated ally Pokémon. If this second usage rallies the same ally Pokémon as the first, the user also flies to that Pokémon and rallies itself.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Rallies the designated ally Pokémon, granting the user and the ally a shield, and additional damage for a single hit for up to 3s. This move can be recast within 3s to rally a second designated ally Pokémon, however the user will not gain a shield this time. Alternatively the user can target themselves, however the recast must be used on an allied Pokémon. If the designated ally is Latios, then Latios will additionally gain 20% movement speed for 2s.

When this move is recast; Latias will fly towards the designated ally. This move's cooldown begins once the recast is used or expires. The damage increase is based on the rallied Pokémon's level.

Upgrade (Level 12): Increased the strength of the shield.

moves.name: Swift
moves.description: Has the user shoot star-shaped rays across a large area in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Shoots star-shaped rays in a large area in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect.

moves.name: Dragon Pulse
moves.description: After the user learns this move, it gains Eon power whenever it defeats wild Pokémon or knocks out Pokémon on the opposing team. This move has the user generate a beam in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and restoring the user's HP. If the user hits an opposing Pokémon with this move, the user gains Eon power. The move also releases telekinetic projectiles which deal damage. The more Eon power the user has accumulated, the more projectiles are released and the more damage they deal.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
After learning this move: Latias gains 2 Eon power for knocking out a wild Pokémon; or 2 Eon power per Pokémon from the opposing team hit by this move. When this move is used: Latias generates a beam in the designated direction, sweeping in a cone shape, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and restoring the user's HP once. While firing the beam, Latias moves 60% slower.

This move gains 2 telekinetic projectiles at 25 and 100 Eon power, and gains 1 projectile at 50 and 75 Eon power for a total of 6 projectiles. Subsequent projectiles hitting the same target deal 15% less damage; further decreasing with each projectile to a maximum of 75% less damage. For every 1 Eon power accumulated beyond 100; the projectile damage increases by 0.5% (Eon power caps at 1099).

Upgrade (Level 10): The beam now slows opposing Pokémon by 30% for 2s

moves.name: Dragon Breath
moves.description: After the user learns this move, it gains Eon power whenever it defeats wild Pokémon or knocks out Pokémon on the opposing team. This move has the user exhale a mighty gust in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and decreasing their Sp. Def. If this move hits Pokémon on the opposing team, the user gains Eon power. The more Eon power the user has accumulated, the greater this move's attack range and damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
After learning this move: Latias gains 2 Eon power for knocking out a wild Pokémon; or 3 Eon power per Pokémon from the opposing team hit by this move. When this move is used: Latias exhales a mighty gust in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon hit, slowing them by 30% for 3s and decreasing their Sp. Def by 30% for 3s.

This move gains increased range at 31 and 61 Eon Power. For every 1 Eon power accumulated beyond 60; the damage increases by 0.5% (Eon power caps at 1059).

Upgrade (Level 10): The slow is increased to 40% for 3s

moves.name: Mist Blast
moves.description: Has the user aim at the designated opposing Pokémon and charge power before releasing a pulse, dealing damage. The user's move cooldowns immediately reset and are reduced for a short time. If Latios is also aiming its Unite Move at the designated opposing Pokémon, the pulses stack and explode, dealing damage around the target as well.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Aims at the designated Pokémon from the opposing team and charges power for 1.8s before releasing a pulse, damaging all opposing Pokémon hit. Resets move cooldowns immediately after releasing the pulse, and increases cooldown reduction by 20% for 9.5s.

If Latios is also aiming its Unite Move at the designated Pokémon from the opposing team, the pulses stack and explode, dealing damage around the target as well. If Mist Blast hits last then the explosion uses Latias' stats.

passiveAbility.name: Levitate
passiveAbility.description: After using a move: increases movement speed by 50% for 2s (decaying by 5% every 0.25s).
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
After using a move: increases movement speed by 50% for 2s (decaying by 5% every 0.25s).

Increases movement speed by 10% while near an ally Latios. When not in combat, this Ability can be used to fly to an allied Latios. This Ability goes on cooldown for 90s after it's triggered for both Latios and Latias. While channeling this ability the user can move around, once the 2s channel is completed the user becomes unstoppable and flies to where Latios was when this ability was activated. If Latios is at the home base, the cooldown of Levitate goes down twice as fast for Latias.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Shoots a psychic projectile at an opposing Pokémon, damaging them.

Latios has no boosted attack.

moves.name: Confusion
moves.description: Has the user release a weak telekinetic force in the designated direction, dealing damage and applying a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Releases a weak telekinetic force in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 2s.

moves.name: Luster Purge
moves.description: Has the user let loose a ball of light. After a set amount of time passes, or if this move is used again, the ball's light releases all at once, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and marking them. The user and any Latias on the user's team deal increased damage to marked opposing Pokémon.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Lets loose a ball of light that moves in the designated direction. When used again (automatically after 0.85s): releases the ball's light all at once, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect; and marking them for 3s. This move then goes on cooldown.

Latios and any allied Latias deal 20% increased damage to marked opposing Pokémon. This mark can be cleansed.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increased mark duration to 6s.

moves.name: Telekinesis
moves.description: Has the user unleash psychic power in the designated direction, dealing damage and applying a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon it hits. If the user hits an opposing Pokémon with this move, the move can be used again to unleash psychic power at a designated opposing Pokémon, dealing damage to it. If the second Pokémon hit by this move is different from the first, the two Pokémon are pulled toward each other and are left unable to act if they collide. If the second Pokémon hit by this move is the same as the first, it is left unable to act.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Unleashes psychic power in the designated direction, damaging the first opposing Pokémon hit, decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 2s; and marking the target for 4s. This move may be used again afterwards. When used again: unleashes psychic power at the designated opposing Pokémon, decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 2s when it hits. This move goes on cooldown when the mark is consumed or cleansed.

If the target of the second use is already marked by the first use or the first use missed: stuns that target for 1s when the psychic power hits, consuming the mark. If the target of the second use is a different, unmarked opposing Pokémon: the two targets are pulled a set distance toward each other over 0.7s. The two Pokémon are stunned for 1s while being pulled together. If they collide with one another, they are thrown away from each other and their marks are consumed.

Upgrade (Level 13): Strengthened movement speed decrease duration to 3s.

moves.name: Swift
moves.description: Has the user shoot star-shaped rays across a large area in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Shoots star-shaped rays in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect.

moves.name: Dragon Pulse
moves.description: After the user learns this move, it gains Eon power whenever it defeats wild Pokémon or knocks out Pokémon on the opposing team. This move has the user dash in the designated direction and makes its next basic attack a boosted attack. If the user hits an opposing Pokémon with a boosted basic attack, the user gains Eon power. This boosted attack also releases telekinetic projectiles, which deal damage. The more Eon power the user has accumulated, the more projectiles are released and damage they deal.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
After learning this move: Latios gains 2 Eon power for knocking out a wild Pokémon; or 3 Eon power per Pokémon from the opposing team hit by this move's boosted attack. When this move is used: Latios dashes forward, empowering the next basic attack used within 5s to be a special boosted attack that hits in a straight line, damaging opposing Pokémon hit. The move goes on cooldown when the boosted attack is used or after 5s.

This move gains 2 telekinetic projectiles at 25 and 100 Eon power, and gains 1 projectile at 50 and 75 Eon power for a total of 6 projectiles. Subsequent projectiles hitting the same target deal 15% less damage; further decreasing with each projectile to a maximum of 75% less damage. For every 1 Eon power accumulated beyond 100; the projectile damage increases by 0.5% (Eon power caps at 1099).

Upgrade (Level 11): The telekinetic projectiles deal an additional 3% missing HP damage to the target.

moves.name: Draco Meteor
moves.description: After the user learns this move, it gains Eon power whenever it defeats wild Pokémon or knocks out Pokémon on the opposing team. This move has the user summon down comet s onto the designated area, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon they hit. If this move hits Pokémon on the opposing team, the user gains Eon power. The more Eon power the user has accumulated, the move comets are summoned and the more damage they deal.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
After learning this move: Latios gains 2 Eon power for knocking out a wild Pokémon; or 3 Eon power per Pokémon from the opposing team hit during this move. When this move is used: 2 comets crash onto the designated area, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect with each comet.

This move gains 1 additional comet for every 25 Eon power accumulated, with a maximum of 6 comets at 100 Eon power. Subsequent comets that hit the same target deal 50% less damage. For every 1 Eon power accumulated beyond 100: increases comet damage by 0.5% (Eon power caps at 1099).

Upgrade (Level 11): Comets deal an additional 3% missing HP damage to opposing Pokémon hit.

moves.name: Eon Blast
moves.description: Has the user aim at the designated opposing Pokémon and charge power before releasing a pulse, dealing damage. The user's move cooldowns immediately reset and are reduced for a short time. If Latias is also aiming its Unite Move at the designated opposing Pokémon, the pulses stack and explode, dealing damage around the target as well.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Aims at the designated Pokémon from the opposing team and charges power for 1.8s before releasing a pulse, damaging all opposing Pokémon hit. Resets move cooldowns immediately after releasing the pulse, and increases cooldown reduction to 60% for 9.5s.

If Latias is also aiming its Unite Move at the designated Pokémon from the opposing team, the pulses stack and explode, dealing damage around the target as well. If Eon Blast hits last then the explosion uses Latios' stats.

passiveAbility.name: Levitate
passiveAbility.description: After using a move: increases movement speed by 50% for 2s (decaying by 5% every 0.25s).
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
After using a move: increases movement speed by 50% for 2s (decaying by 5% every 0.25s).

Increases movement speed by 10% while near an ally Latias. When not in combat, this Ability can be used to fly to an allied Latias. This Ability goes on cooldown for 90s after it's triggered for both Latios and Latias. While channeling this ability the user can move around, once the 2s channel is completed the user becomes unstoppable and flies to where Latias was when this ability was activated. If Latias is at home base, the cooldown of Levitate will go down twice as fast for Latios.

moves.name: Attack - Eevee
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing additional damage.

moves.name: Swift
moves.description: Has the user shoot star-shaped rays, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon they hit.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Launches three stars in the designated direction in a cone, dealing damage to enemies hit. The first star that hits applies a debuff that lasts 1 second that causes subsequent stars to deal 70% reduced damage.

moves.name: Razor Leaf
moves.description: Has the user launch five sharp-edged leaves in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon they hit before sticking into the ground for a short time. If this move is used again, the leaves return to the user and deal damage to opposing Pokémon they hit along the way. If the leaves are returned to the user after a set amount of time has passed since they stuck into the ground, they deal increased damage. If all five leaves hit one Pokémon, the user is granted a shield.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Launches a fan of five sharp-edged leaves in the designated direction in a cone, dealing damage to enemies hit before sticking into the ground for 2.5s. If this move is activated again, the leaves return, dealing damage to Pokémon they hit along the way. If the reactivation time is 1s or longer, the leaves will return and deal increased damage. If the move is not reactivated, the leaves will automatically return dealing increased damage. If five leaves hit the same Pokémon (outgoing or incoming) within 0.5s, Leafeon is granted a shield lasting 1.5s. Leafeon also receives 20% increased movement speed for up to 3s while the leaves are out. Each fan, the first leaf that hits applies a debuff that lasts 1s and causes subsequent leaves within the same fan (outgoing or incoming) to deal 70% reduced damage.

Upgrade (Level 10): Increase the strength of the shield when five leaves hit.

moves.name: Solar Blade
moves.description: Has the user start gathering light and filling a blade with the light's energy. If this move is used again after the user has started gathering light, the user brings the blade down, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. The longer the blade is filled with light energy, the more damage this move deals. When the user's Chlorophyll gauge is full, the cooldown for the next use of this move is reduced, and when the move is used, the blade is brought down immediately and deals maximum damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Start gathering light and filling a blade with the light's energy. If this move is used again after light has been gathered, Leafeon brings the blade down in the designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon it hits. The longer the blade is filled with light energy, the more damage is dealt. If this move is used when the Chlorophyll gauge is full, the blade is brought down immediately, dealing maximum damage and the cooldown for the next use of this move is reduced by 50%. The cooldown of this move is also reduced by 50% upon the Chlorophyll gauge being filled.

Upgrade (Level 10): While charging, Leafeon receives 50% damage reduction.

moves.name: Quick Attack
moves.description: Has the user move quickly in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it makes contact with along the way.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Dash in a direction, dealing damage to enemies hit.

moves.name: Aerial Ace
moves.description: Has the user move with speed and slash, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. The user's next basic attack after using this move deals increased damage. A maximum of two uses can be kept in reserve for this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Dash in a direction, dealing damage to enemies hit. The next attack is a boosted attack non-Chlorophyll empowered. A maximum of two uses may be kept in reserve for this move and cannot be used more than once every 0.5s.

Upgrade (Level 12): Reduces the cooldown for charges by 0.5s.

moves.name: Leaf Blade
moves.description: Has the user dash and then spin while slashing around itself with sharp leaves, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. If the outer ring of this move's area of effect hits opposing Pokémon, it deals increased damage, decreases the opposing Pokémon's movement speed, and charges the user's Chlorophyll gauge.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Dash and spin in a direction, slashing around itself and with a ring of sharp leaves, dealing damage three times. Enemies caught by the outside edge of the ring of sharp leaves take increased damage and are slowed by 40% for 2s. Enemies damaged by the outside edge also charge the Chlorophyll gauge by 1.2s per hit.

Upgrade (Level 12): Reduces this move's cooldown by 1.2s for each hit dealing damage to more than one enemy on the outer ring

moves.name: Emerald Two-Step
moves.description: Has the user leap at the designated opposing Pokémon, dealing damage to it and decreasing its movement speed. After that, the user leaps up and lands with force, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. Those opposing Pokémon take more damage the closer they are to the center of the area of effect.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Leaps at a designated enemy, dealing damage to them and all enemies around them. These Pokémon are additionally slowed by 50% for 2s. After the initial leap, Leafeon leaps up and lands with force dealing damage to enemies in an area. Enemies hit by this area take more damage the closer they are to the center of this area. Leafeon is unstoppable during this move.

passiveAbility.name: Run Away
passiveAbility.description: Eevee has 10% increased movement speed when out of combat. (5s after leaving combat)

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, performing a combo attack with a bone. Deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and throws them.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with increased range every third attack, hitting twice. The first hit damages opposing Pokémon with a normal basic attack; the second hit is a follow-up attack with a bone, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area a second time and throwing them for 0.5s.

moves.name: Quick Attack
moves.description: Has the user lunge forward at a speed that makes it almost invisible, dealing damage to enemies it comes in contact with.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Dashes forward, damaging opposing Pokémon hit.

moves.name: Extreme Speed
moves.description:
Charge forward with breathtaking speed. If this move hits an enemy from point-blank range, its cooldown is reset and it restores the user's HP. However, the cooldown cannot be continually reset by using this move on the same enemy multiple times in succession.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases Attack for a short time when this move is used.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Dashes forward, damaging opposing Pokémon hit; restoring HP for each opposing Pokémon hit. If an auto attack is used at the end of the dash (0.2s after the move is used): attacks in a circle around them, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect with a unique auto attack.

After learning this move: nearby opposing Pokémon have an Extreme Speed marker automatically applied to them. If this move's dash hits an opposing Pokémon: resets this move's cooldown; and removes the marker from the first target hit for 10s (or until applied by other moves).

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the user's Attack by 7.5% for 2s when this move is used.

moves.name: Power-Up Punch
moves.description:
Charges power before unleashing a powerful attack. While the user is charging power, its movement speed is decreased but its Attack slowly increases and the damage it receives is reduced. The user then charges in the designated direction and releases this move, dealing more damage the lower the enemies remaining HP is. The next basic attack becomes a boosted attack if the move hits. This move's cooldown is reduced every time one of the user's moves hits.

Upgrade (Level 11): Become immune to hindrances while charging power.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
While held down: charges up power for up to 4s, reducing damage received by 30% and decreasing movement speed by 15%; reaches maximum charge after 1s. When released: dashes forward with a devastating punch, damaging the first opposing Pokémon hit, dealing additional missing HP damage to the target, and shoving them for 0.5s. Readies a boosted attack if this move hits. Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s each time one of their moves lands a hit. If not released after 4s or canceled, this move goes on a 1.4s cooldown.

12% of Enemy Missing HP (caps at 1000 dmg).

Against wild Pokémon: additional missing HP damage is capped at 1000.

Upgrade (Level 11): Becomes unstoppable while charging this move.

moves.name: Meteor Mash
moves.description: Blast forward with a punch like a comet, dealing damage to enemies and shoving them.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Punches with the force of a meteor in the designated direction after 0.4s, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and shoving them in the designated direction, stunning those hit for 0.4s.

moves.name: Bone Rush
moves.description:
Performs a combo attack with a bone, dealing multiple damaging blows to enemies and shoving them. The final attack sends the bone flying. If used again while the bone is traveling, the user rushes to the location of the bone, dealing damage to nearby enemies.

Upgrade (Level 13): Using this move again will reset the cooldown for Extreme Speed or Power-Up Punch.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Rapidly strikes with a bone in the designated direction 3 times over 0.33s, damaging opposing Pokémon within melee range around Lucario, and in the area of the bone strikes in front; opposing Pokémon can be damaged twice per hit by being in the area where the bone strikes overlap with the attacks within melee range. Immediately after, throws the bone flying to land a short distance away, shoving opposing Pokémon in the way and stunning them for 0.4s. Cooldown starts upon first use. If used again within 3s of throwing the bone: dashes to the bone, then catches it, damaging nearby opposing Pokémon if the bone is grabbed.

Landing one of the three initial strikes on opposing Pokémon applies an Extreme Speed marker to them.

Upgrade (Level 13): Catching the bone with the second use resets the cooldown of Extreme Speed or Power-Up Punch.

moves.name: Close Combat
moves.description:
Unleash a combo attack, dealing multiple blows to enemies and shoving them.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increased damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes unstoppable for 1.4s, and rapidly punches in the designated direction 11 times, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area in front of them with each hit; restoring HP each time this move hits. The rapid punches start 0.4s after the move is used, with the last punch hitting 1.2s after the start of the move.

Changes the next basic attack used within 5s after using this move. This auto attack gains increased range and lunges at an opposing Pokémon with a kick, dealing damage.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the damage of this move.

moves.name: Aura Cannon
moves.description: Deals damage to enemies in the area of effect. Increases the damage dealt by the user's next Power-Up Punch after using this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Becomes unstoppable for 0.6s and channels aura into a huge sphere, then launches the sphere in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in its path. The aura sphere picks up speed as it travels; at close range, a target can be hit up to 4 times, while at max speed a target will only be hit once.

If the user learned Extreme Speed: applies an Extreme Speed marker to targets hit by this Unite Move. If the user learned Power-Up Punch: strengthens the damage of the next attack of Power-Up Punch by roughly 20% and the shove duration to 1s when Power-Up Punch is released.

passiveAbility.name: Steadfast
passiveAbility.description: While at low HP, gain a shield and increased movement speed. (30s cooldown)
