passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: If at 50% max HP or below: grants this Pokémon a shield and increases their movement speed by 20% for 5s (45s CD).

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with increased range every third attack, hitting twice. The first hit damages opposing Pokémon with a normal basic attack; the second hit is a follow-up attack with a bone, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area a second time and throwing them for 0.5s.

moves.name: Quick Attack
moves.description: Has the user lunge forward at a speed that makes them almost invisible, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon they come in contact with.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Dashes forward, damaging opposing Pokémon hit.

moves.name: Power-Up Punch
moves.description: Charges power before unleashing a powerful attack. While the user is charging power, itsm ovement speed is decreased but its Attack slowly increases and the damage it receives is reduced. The user then charges in the designated direction and releases the move, dealing more damage the lower the opposing Pokémon's remaining HP is and making its own next basic attack a boosted attack if the move hits. This move's cooldown is reduced every time one of the user's move hits. When the user Mega Evolves, it can charge power for a longer duration, deals increased damage to opposing Pokémon, and shoves them.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user receives 30% reduced damage and decreases their movement speed by 15% as they charge up power for up to 4s, reaching maximum charge after 1s; then dashes forward with a devastating punch when released, damaging the first opposing Pokémon hit, dealing additional missing HP damage to the target and shoving them for 0.5s. If this move hits, the user readies a boosted attack. This move's cooldown is reduced by 1s each time one of the user's moves lands a hit. If this move is canceled or not released after 4s, it goes on a 1.4s cooldown.

12% of Enemy Missing HP (caps at 1000 dmg).

When the user is mega evolved; the move can hit multiple opposing Pokémon and can be overcharged for an additional 0.4s to throw all opposing Pokémon it hits for 1s.

Upgrade (Level 11): Becomes unstoppable while charging this move.

moves.name: Meteor Mash
moves.description: Blasts forward with a punch like a comet, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and shoving them.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Punches with the force of a meteor in the designated direction after 0.4s, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and shoving them in the designated direction, stunning those hit for 0.4s.

moves.name: Close Combat
moves.description: Unleashes a combo attack, dealing multiple blows to opposing Pokémon. When this move hits, it restores a certain amount of the user's HP. While using this move, the user becomes immune to hindrances. When the user Mega Evolves, it can deal more consecutive blows and change direction when attacking. If this move is used again, or if Power-Up Punch is used, this move can be canceled. This move's cooldown is reduced every time the user's moves hit.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user becomes unstoppable and rapidly punches in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area in front of them (up to 11 times), and restoring the user's HP each time this move hits. The next auto attack within 5s after using this move gains increased range and causes the user to lunge at an opposing Pokémon with a kick.

When the user is mega evolved; the move lasts longer dealing damage up to 16 times reducing this move's cooldown by 0.4s for each hit on Pokémon from the opposing team, can be slowly rotated, the last hit can be targeted in any direction, and this move can be ended early, after channeling for at least 1s, by activating the move again or by using Power-Up Punch. The first hit of this move has 12x the normal healing. Hitting an enemy with Power-Up punch reduces the cooldown of this move by 1s.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the damage of this move.

moves.name: Aura Cannon
moves.description: Has the user deal damage to opposing Pokmon in the area of effect After using this Unite Move, the user Mega Evolves.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user becomes unstoppable and channels aura into a huge sphere, then launches the sphere in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in its path. The aura sphere picks up speed as it travels; at close range, a target can be hit up to 4 times, while at max speed a target will only be hit once.

After unleashing this move the user mega evolves for 25s adding additional effects to Close Combat and Power-Up Punch and changing the passive ability to Adaptability.

passiveAbility.name: Justified
passiveAbility.description: When the user is damaged, their attack stat increases by 8% for 4s up to 4 times.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: When the user is damaged, their attack stat increases by 8% for 4s up to 4 times.

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage when it hits. This boosted attack also decreases the movement speed of enemies for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage when it hits. This boosted attack also decreases the movement speed of enemies by 50% for 1s.

moves.name: Karate Chop
moves.description: Attack opposing Pokémon with a sharp chop.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Machamp attacks in a straight line dealing damage to enemies hit.

moves.name: Close Combat
moves.description:
Unleash a series of punches in the designated direction, dealing increased damage with the last punch. The user can move while using this move and becomes immune to hindrances.

Upgrade (Level 13): Deals increased damage to enemies that are afflicted by a status condition.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Machamp unleashes a barrage of blows in a targeted direction for 1s, hitting multiple times and finishing with a single large strike. Machamp is unstoppable for the duration and can move while using Close Combat.

Upgrade (Level 13): Deals 25% additional damage if the target is affected by a status condition.

moves.name: Cross Chop
moves.description:
Has the user deliver a double chop with forearms crossed while dashing forward. This increases the user's critical-hit rate.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases Attack with each basic attack, up to a set max.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Machamp dashes forward, dealing damage to enemies hit. Cross Chop may critically hit and has a 5% higher Critical-Hit Rate. Cross Chop further increases Critical-Hit Rate by 5% for 3s on hit.

Upgrade (Level 13): Machamp’s auto attacks increases Atk by 3 per target hit, permanently stacking up to 40 times.

moves.name: Bulk Up
moves.description: Become immune to hindrances for a short time. Increases movement speed, basic attack speed, and Attack. The next basic attack also becomes a boosted attack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Machop buffs themselves for 3s becoming unstoppable, increasing attack by 15%, attack speed by 40% and movement speed by 30% inititally, diminishing gradually every 1s. Machop's next attack is boosted.

moves.name: Dynamic Punch
moves.description:
Jump to the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and leaving them unable to act when it hits. After using this move, the user becomes immune to hindrances, their movement speed and basic attack speed is increased. The next basic attack also becomes a boosted attack.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increased Attack and movement speed.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Machamp jumps to target location, becoming unstoppable for 0.5s, stunning enemies hit for 0.5s, slowing them by 40% for 3s, and dealing damage four times in quick succession to the landing radius. Machamp’s Attack is increased by 15% for 5s and gains bonus movement speed of 40% diminishing by 20% over 3s when using Dynamic Punch. Machamp’s next auto attack is boosted.

Upgrade (Level 11): Further increases movement speed by 30%, Attack bonus by 5%, and slow by 20%.

moves.name: Submission
moves.description:
Increases movement speed and become immune to hindrances. When the next basic attack hits, the attack deals increased damage and slams the enemy onto the ground, leaving it unable to act. Once this move ends, the user's critical-hit rate and basic attack speed are increased for a short time.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increased critical-hit rate and basic attack speed.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Machamp becomes unstoppable for 5s and gains 70% increased movement speed (diminishing every 1s by 20%) or until the next auto attack. Machamp’s next auto attack during submission is enhanced, hitting twice and slamming the target down behind Machamp. If this attack hits, Machamp also gains 5% increased Critical-Hit Rate and 40% Attack Speed for 3s.

Upgrade (Level 11): Further increases Critical-Hit Rate by 5% and Attack Speed by 10%.

moves.name: Barrage Blow
moves.description: Increases Machamp's movement speed by 5%, Attack by 30%, Defense by 200, and Sp. Defense by 200 for 10s. If used again, unleash a combo attack, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and shoving them. The final blow deals double the damage of the previous hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Machamp gains increased movement speed by 10%, Attack by 25%, Defense by 300, and Sp. Defense by 300 for 8s before unleashing an unstoppable combo attack that continuously shoves enemies in the area of effect.

passiveAbility.name: Guts
passiveAbility.description: Increases Attack when afflicted by a status condition.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: Increases Attack by 10% for 5s when afflicted by any debuff.

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing damage and freezing enemies for a short time when it hits. Mamoswine's basic attacks deal increased damage for a short time to enemies recently frozen with one of Mamoswine's moves.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing damage and freezing enemies for 0.5s when it hits. Freezing an enemy causes the user’s auto attacks to deal increased damage to them for 3s. Reapplying a freeze will refresh this duration.

moves.name: Ice Shard
moves.description: Hurls 3 chunks of ice at the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. If an enemy is hit by all three chunks, they're left frozen for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Hurls 3 chunks of ice at the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed by 40% for 1.5s. If an enemy is hit by all three chunks, they're left frozen for 0.5s.

moves.name: Icicle Crash
moves.description:
Drops icicles on the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. The final icicle shatters after a set amount of time or when hit by Mamoswine's other moves or boosted attacks, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and leaving them frozen. If the icicle is shattered by Tackle or High Horsepower, the ground is covered in ice, which increases the movement speed of allies that run over it. The next basic attack is boosted.

Upgrade (Level 11): The final icicle leaves enemies hit unable to act for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Drops icicles on the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed by 35% for 0.2s. The final icicle shatters after 1s or when hit by Mamoswine's other moves or boosted attacks, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and leaving them frozen for 1.5s if the icicle was shattered by Mamoswine, or 1s if it shattered on its own. If the icicle is shattered by Tackle or High Horsepower, the ground is covered in ice, which increases the movement speed of allies that run over it by 50%. The next auto attack is boosted.

Upgrade (Level 11): The final icicle leaves enemies hit unable to act for .2s.

moves.name: Ice Fang
moves.description:
Uses cold-infused fangs to throw the opposing Pokémon closest to the user, dealing damage and leaving them frozen for a short time. The user can then designate a direction to slam the thrown Pokémon forward or backward, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and leaving them frozen for a short time. The next basic attack is boosted.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the area of effect in which enemies are left frozen after the slam attack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Uses cold-infused fangs to throw the enemy closest to Mamoswine, dealing damage and grabbing them, rendering them unable to act. Mamoswine can then designate a direction to slam the thrown Pokémon forward or backward, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and freezing them. The thrown Pokémon is frozen for 1s, and surrounding enemies are frozen for 1.5s. The next auto attack is boosted.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the area of effect in which enemies are left frozen after the slam attack.

moves.name: Tackle
moves.description: Mamoswine rushes recklessly in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies they hit and throws them in the air.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Mamoswine rushes recklessly in the designated direction; dealing damage to enemies hit and throws them in the air for 0.5s.

moves.name: High Horsepower
moves.description:
Mamoswine rushes recklessly in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies they hit and shove them. Mamoswine then stomps their feet, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and leaves them unable to act for a short time. This stomping deals increased damage to frozen enemies.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the reckless rush's speed and the length of time enemies are left unable to act.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mamoswine rushes recklessly in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies they hit and shoving them for 0.55s. Mamoswine then stomps their feet, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and leaves them unable to act for 0.35s. This stomping deals increased damage to frozen enemies.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the reckless rush's speed and the length of time enemies are left unable to act by .2s.

moves.name: Earthquake
moves.description:
Mamoswine leaps up in the designated direction and lands forcefully, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and pulling them toward Mamoswine.

Upgrade (Level 13): Decreases the movement speed of enemies for a short time when this move hits and increases the move's pulling power.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mamoswine leaps up in the designated direction and lands forcefully, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and pulling them toward Mamoswine. Enemies that are hit cannot act for 1s.

Upgrade (Level 13): Decreases movement speed of enemies by 30% for 2.5s when this move hits and increases the move's pulling power.

moves.name: Mammoth Mash
moves.description: Mamoswine jumps to the designated location and becomes immune to hindrances - then repeatedly stomps the ground, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their movement speed for a short time with each stomp. The final stomp throws enemies in the air.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Mamoswine jumps to the designated location then repeatedly stomps the ground four times, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their movement speed for 0.5s with each stomp. The first stomp slows enemies by 30%, the two mini stomps slow them by 20%, and the final stomp slows them by 40% and throws them in the air for 1.5s. Mamoswine is unstoppable for the duration of the move.

passiveAbility.name: Thick Fat
passiveAbility.description: When Mamoswine deals damage, Defense and Sp. Def are increased for 3s, stacking up to 3 times. When Mamoswine uses Ice Shard, Icicle Crash or Ice Fang, the next basic attack is boosted. If any of these moves creates a freezing area of effect inside an ally goal zone, the goal-scoring speed of enemies is decreased.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: When Mamoswine deals damage, Defense and Sp. Def are increased for 5s, stacking up to 3 times. When Mamoswine uses Ice Shard, Icicle Crash or Ice Fang, the next auto attack is boosted. If any of these moves creates a freezing area of effect inside an ally goal zone, the goal-scoring speed of enemies is decreased by 30% for 5s.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user whips their vines about in a headbutt, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon.

This Pokémon has no boosted attack.

moves.name: Magical Leaf
moves.description: Has the user scatter curious leaves, dealing damage and applying a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user scatters curious leaves at a designated target, dealing damage and slowing opposing Pokémon by 40% for 2s.

moves.name: Grass Knot
moves.description: Has the user slash its vines in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and leaving them unable to move. When this move becomes a full bloom move, the user's vines grow thicker. After this move hits an opposing Pokémon, vines will also spread around it and ensnare other nearby opposing Pokémon, leaving them unable to move after a set amount of time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user slashes with their vines in the designated direction, stopping at the first target hit, dealing damage and rooting opposing Pokémon for 1s.

While in full bloom, the move becomes wider. After hitting an opposing Pokémon, other nearby opposing Pokémon will be targeted, after 2s have passed any targeted Pokémon in range will be hit by this move.

Upgrade (Level 11): Reduces the cooldown of this move by 1s.

moves.name: Synthesis
moves.description: Has the user produce energy through photosynthesis in the area surrounding a designated Pokémon, recovering the HP of ally Pokémon in the area of effect. For a short time, opposing Pokémon in the area of effect deal decreased damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user photosynthesizes and produces energy around the designated ally and the user for 1s. Allies in the area of effect get healed, doubly so if within both circles of energy, while opposing Pokémon have their damage lowered by 15% for 3s.

moves.name: Petal Blizzard
moves.description: Has the user stir up a violent petal blizzard in the designated direction, dealing damage and applying a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon while restoring ally Pokémon HP within its area of effect. The HP of ally Pokémon near to the user will also be restored. When this move becomes a full bloom move, the blizzard's area of effect is increased, and the petals remain for a set amount of time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user stirs up a violent petal blizzard in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and slowing them down by 30% for 2s, while an area around the user heals ally Pokémon.

15% of the first hit of damage.

5% of the first tick of healing.

While in full bloom, the blizzard travels further and leaves a lingering area for 2s dealing damage every 0.5s up to 4 more times and slowing opposing Pokémon by 40% for 2s instead. The user and ally Pokémon near the user are healed up to 4 more times every 0.5s, additionally walking through the lingering area grants 50% increased movement speed for 2s. Damage and healing beyond the first instance has reduced effectiveness.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the effectiveness of this move's healing.

moves.name: Full Bloom Aroma
moves.description: Has the user emit a sweet aroma from the flowers around its neck, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon. The fragrance also soothes the urge to fight, decreasing the damage dealt by nearby opposing Pokémon. While the sweet smell lingers, the user's next move will be boosted, becoming a full bloom move. When a move hits opposing Pokémon, the Unite move gauge charges.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user emits a sweet aroma around themselves dealing damage to opposing Pokémon every 0.5s up to 10 times. Opposing Pokémon hit by this move have their damage reduced by 15% for 3s, the duration is refreshed on each hit. For up to 5s, the user's next move will be in full bloom, granting extra effects.

Meganium gains 2700 energy per second instead of the usual 900. Hitting with moves grants 4500 energy.

passiveAbility.name: Overgrow
passiveAbility.description: The user heals an additional 10% of the healing value to allies or themself while that target is at or below 30% HP.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: The user heals an additional 10% of the healing value to allies or themself while that target is at or below 30% HP.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Gains a boosted attack every 5 seconds that leaps towards the target dealing additional damage.

moves.name: Leafage
moves.description: Has the user shoot out leaves that deal damage to opposing Pokémon they hit and decrease their movement speed.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Launch 4 leaves that deal damage to enemies hit and decrease their movement speed by 50% for 2.5s. When an enemy is hit by a leaf, subsequent leaves deal 20% reduced damage.

moves.name: Flower Trick
moves.description: Has the user attach a flower bomb to an opposing Pokémon. The flower bomb can be detonated at will if the move is used again, and when it explodes, it deals damage based on the percentage of HP the opposing Pokémon has lost. If the user deals damage to an opposing Pokémon that has a flower bomb attached to it, the explosion's area of effect is increased and it deals increased damage. If the user knocks out an opposing Pokémon with this move, the move's cooldown is reset.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Attach a flower bomb to an enemy that lasts up to 5s. This move can be used again to detonate the flower bomb at will. When it explodes it deals damage and additional damage equal to 18% of the target's missing HP. Against Wild Pokémon: The additional damage from missing HP is capped at 1000. If an enemy that has a flower bomb attached to them is damaged by Meowscarada, the explosion's area of effect and damage are increased. If the explosion knocks out an enemy player with this move, the move's cooldown is reset.

18% Missing HP

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the range of application and explosion's area of effect. Also increases movement speed when planting the seed by 30% for 3s diminishing by 10% every 1s.

moves.name: Night Slash
moves.description: Has the user throw slashing waves with its claws, dealing damage and applying a mark to opposing Pokémon the slashing waves hit. The user's basic attacks have an increased critical-hit rate on marked opposing Pokémon. When the user deals damage to marked opposing Pokémon with basic attacks, this move's cooldown is reduced and the user recovers HP.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The users throws 3 slashing waves with their claws, dealing damage and applying a mark for 5s to enemies hit. Subsequent waves deal 70% damage of the first. Basic attacks by Meowscarada have 50% increased critical-hit rate on marked enemies. When Meowscarada deals damage to marked enemies with basic attacks, this move's cooldown is reduced by 0.5s and Meowscarada recovers HP. The HP recovery works in a cycle of 3 basic attacks; the first attack will heal 100% of the value, the second 50% and the third 20%, then the fourth will heal 100% again.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the critical-hit rate on marked enemies to a 65% increase and restores additional HP from hitting marked targets.

moves.name: Hone Claws
moves.description: Has the user sharpen its claws while moving, increasing the speed and damage of its next three basic attacks.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Dash in a designated direction while sharpening claws, increasing attack speed by 80% and increasing the damage of the next three basic attacks for 3s.

moves.name: Double Team
moves.description: Has the user create an illusory copy of itself while dashing in order to befuddle opposing Pokémon. If this move is used again, the user swaps places with the illusory copy. When this swap happens, or when the illusory copy's HP drops to 0, flower petals are scattered around, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and leaving them unable to act for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Dash and create an illusory copy in order to befuddle enemies. The clone has the same HP and defenses as Meowscarada but takes 30% more damage and lasts for 3.3s. If this move is used again, the user swaps places with the illusory copy, dispelling the copy and creating an explosion of petals at both the clone and Meowscarada's positions that deal damage and leave enemies hit unable to act for 0.8s. Enemies may only take damage from one petal explosion. If the clone's HP were to reach 0 before a swap, the same type of petal explosion occurs at the clone's location only. Creating the clone also increases Meowscarada's movement speed by 70% for 3s diminishing by 10% every 0.5s. Cooldown starts when first used.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the duration of the clone to 4.6s and sets the increased damage the clone receives to 20%.

moves.name: Trailblaze
moves.description: Has the user leap to the designated location, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and leaving them paralyzed. If the user hits opposing Pokémon with this move, its basic attack speed and movement speed increase for a short time. When the user is in tall grass, this move's range increases.< If the user knocks out an opposing Pokémon or makes an assist, this move's cooldown is reset.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Leap to the designated location, dealing damage to enemies hit and leaving them paralyzed, decreasing their attack speed by 40% and movement speed by 40% for 2s. If Meowscarada hits enemies with this move, Meowscarada's basic attack speed is increased by 100% for 4s and movement speed is increased by 60% for 4s diminishing by 5% every 0.5s down to minimum of 35%. When used from tall grass, this move's leap range increases. If Meowscarada knocks out an enemy player or makes an assist, this move's cooldown is reset.

Upgrade (Level 13): Grants a shield that lasts 3 seconds when used.

moves.name: Floral Flourish
moves.description: Has the user move instantly to the designated location, creating a whirlwind of flowers around itself that continually deals damage to opposing Pokémon. While using this move, the user cannot use other attacks, and the move can be cancelled if it is used again.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Move instantly to the designated location, creating a whirlwind around Meowscarada for 3s that continually deals damage to enemies every 0.25s, up to 13 times. During the blink, Meowsacarda is Invincible. Then, Meowscarada will slowly walk forward with the storm. While using this move, the user cannot use other attacks or change directions without the use of Eject Button, and the move can be stopped if it is used again. Hits on enemy Pokémon reduce the cooldown of Night Slash and Flower Trick by 1 second per enemy hit.

passiveAbility.name: Overgrow
passiveAbility.description: When the user is at 50% max HP or less, if they would take damage they instead disappear into leaves, becoming invisible for 1.5s or until attacking (60s CD). This effect also increases the user's movement speed by 15%, damage by 15% and heals by 15% of the damage dealt for 4s.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: When the user is at 50% max HP or less, if they would take damage they instead disappear into leaves, becoming invisible for 1.5s or until attacking (60s CD). This effect also increases the user's movement speed by 15%, damage by 15% and heals by 15% of the damage dealt for 4s.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Scratches at opposing Pokémon becoming a boosted attack with every third attack dealing increased damage and dropping a gold coin.

moves.name: Scratch
moves.description: Has the user scratch a designated opposing Pokémon with its sharp claws, dealing damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user scratches a designated Pokémon with its sharp claws, dealing damage.

moves.name: Pay Day
moves.description: Has the user hurl a gold coin in the designated direction, dealing damage and applying a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon it hits. Gold coins will drop near opposing Pokémon it hits. A maximum of {0} use(s) can be kept in reserve for this move. Picking up gold coins reduces the time until another reserve use becomes available.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user hurls a gold coin at the designated Pokémon, dealing damage, slowing down the target by 30% for 1.5s, and dropping a gold coin.

2 uses can be kept in reserve, picking up a gold coin reduces the cooldown of a use by 4s.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the damage dealt by this move.

moves.name: Fury Swipes
moves.description: Has the user swipe twice with its sharp claws at the designated opposing Pokémon, dealing damage and granting the user a shield effect. The number of swipes increases (up to 5 maximum) based on the number of times the user has recently dealt damage to opposing Pokémon. When this move hits, that number of times resets. If the maximum number of swipes hit, the cooldowns for Feint, Feint Attack, and Assurance reset.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user applies a coin mark on opposing Pokémon they hit, the coin mark has up to 5 stacks and lasts for 5s. The user swipes twice with its sharp claws at the designated Pokémon, dealing damage, and granting the user a shield for 5s, and an additional shield per hit (up to 5x) for 5s. The number of swipes can be increased up to 5 if the coin mark is at 3 stacks or higher. Subsequent swipes deal half of the damage of the first hit. This attack will stun opposing Pokémon that have 1/2/3+ coin mark stacks for 0.3/0.5/0.8s respectively.

If the maximum number of swipes hit, the cooldowns for Feint, Feint Attack, and Assurance are reduced by 7.5% of the max cooldown per hit, the 5th hit will fully reset the cooldown. If the coin mark is filled up, this move will consume and reset the mark.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases the damage dealt by this move.

moves.name: Feint
moves.description: Has the user dash at the designated opposing Pokémon, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user dashes at the designated Pokémon, dealing damage to opponents hit.

moves.name: Feint Attack
moves.description: Has the user pounce at the designated opposing Pokémon and scratch them with its claws, dealing damage and leaving them unable to act. When this move hits, the user is granted a shield effect and can quickly move in a designated direction. The user's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user pounces at the designated Pokémon and scratches them with their claws, dealing damage and stunning the target for 0.6s. When this move hits the user is granted a shield for 3s and can quickly move in a chosen direction.

The user's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack. If the coin mark is filled up, this move will consume and reset the mark (although this move does not benefit from the coin mark in any fashion).

Upgrade (Level 13): Improves the strength of the shield.

moves.name: Assurance
moves.description: Has the user charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and restoring the user's HP. The more times the user has recently dealt damage to the opposing Pokémon (maximum 5 times), the more HP is restored. This move deals double damage to opposing Pokémon the user has recently dealt damage to five or more times. The count of times then resets.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user applies a coin mark on opposing Pokémon they hit, the coin mark has up to 5 stacks and lasts for 5s. The user charges in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit and restoring the user's HP, furthermore the move will restore additional HP if the targets have a coin mark. The more stacks the coin mark has the more potent the heal.

When this move is used on a Pokémon with a coin mark that has 5 stacks, the damage is doubled. If the coin mark is filled up, this move will consume and reset the mark.

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduces this moves cooldown by 1s.

moves.name: Gold Coin Barrage
moves.description: Has the user unleash a barrage of gold coins toward the designated Pokémon from the opposing team, dealing damage. If Pokémon from the opposing team step in the coins' path, the coins will hit them instead. During the attack, coins will periodically drop near Pokémon from the opposing team hit by the move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user becomes unstoppable and unleashes a barrage of gold coins toward the designated Pokémon from the opposing team, dealing damage in a small area up to 10 times. If another Pokémon on the opposing team stands in the way, they will be hit instead. During the attack, up to 4 coins will periodically drop near Pokémon hit by this move. This move can be cancelled early.

Meowth can move while using this move, but is slowed by 110%.

passiveAbility.name: Pickup
passiveAbility.description: Attacking Pokémon from the opposing team copies their battle item for 30s. Defeating a wild Pokémon instead copies a random battle item for 30s. The copied item temporarily functions as the user’s battle item, without replacing or affecting the user’s original item. Once an item is copied, this ability will go on cooldown for 30s.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
Attacking Pokémon from the opposing team copies their battle item for 30s. Defeating a wild Pokémon instead copies a random battle item for 30s. The copied item temporarily functions as the user’s battle item, without replacing or affecting the user’s original item. Once an item is copied, this ability will go on cooldown for 30s.

Moving towards Aeos energy or gold coins increases the user's movement speed by 20%. Picking up gold coins increases the user's movement speed for 3s by 40% decaying by 5% every 0.5s to a minimum of 20%, and speeds up the rate at which Pickup's cooldown goes down by 13.5% for each gold coin picked up. Gold coins will stay on the field for 7s. The user can pick up an opposing Meowth's gold coins as if they were their own.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, striking with an increased area of attack. When a boosted attack hits, the cooldown of Iron Defense, Meteor Mash or Gyro Ball is reduced by 10%. If multiple targets are hit, the cooldown is instead reduced by 20%.

moves.name: Iron Defense
moves.description: The user is granted a shield, and the user's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Gain a shield lasting 5s and gain a boosted attack. That boosted attack deals additional damage.

moves.name: Meteor Mash
moves.description: Has the user charge power in its fist, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in an area of effect while granting itself a shield. The more opposing Pokémon this move hits, the more the shield effect is strengthened and the more damage the user deals with its next basic attack This next basic attack hits with a hard punch fired like a meteor, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in an area of effect.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user charges power in their fist, dealing damage to enemies in an area up to 4x and slowing them by 20% for 3s while granting the user a shield for 5s. Enemies may dodge the charging damage by moving out of the area. The more enemies that this move hits, the more shield the user gains (stacking up to 8 times) and the more damage is dealt with the next basic attack. The next basic attack hits with a hard punch fired like a meteor, dealing damage to enemies in an area of effect. The level of charge depends on how many hits were made during the charging.

Upgrade (Level 11): Restores HP when the punch hits enemies.

moves.name: Gyro Ball
moves.description: Has the user spin rapdily, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon. When the outer edge of the spin hits an opposing Pokémon, the user is granted a shield. If this move hits many opposing Pokémon, oor hits Pokémon on the opposing team, the shield effect becomes stronger.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Spin rapidly, dealing damage to nearby enemies. Hitting enemies with the outer edge of the spin grants a shield to the user for 4s. The shield stacks based on the number of targets hit up to a maximum of 6 times, with wild Pokémon granting 1 stack and enemy players granting 3 stacks.

Upgrade (Level 11): On expiration of shield duration, converts 30% of the remaining shield to healing.

moves.name: Tackle
moves.description: Has the user scan to see if there is an opposing Pokémon in front of it. If there is, the user charges toward the opposing Pokémon it finds, dealing damage to it.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Scan in a designated direction. If the scan hits an enemy, the user then charges toward the enemy hit, dealing damage.

moves.name: Zen Headbutt
moves.description: Has the user fire a psychic wave in a forward direction, then charge toward opposing Pokémon it hits, dealing damage and throwing them.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Fires a psychic wave in a designated direction. If the move hits an enemy, the user then charges toward the enemy hit, dealing damage and throwing them for 0.8s.

Upgrade (Level 13): After dealing damage with Zen Headbutt, reduce incoming damage by 20% for 2s.

moves.name: Magnet Rise
moves.description: Has the user levitate for a set amount of time using electrically-generated magnetism, increasing the user's movement speed. If the user performs a basic attack while levitating, the user falls on the ground forcefully, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon and throwing them.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Levitates for up to 4s using electrically-generated magnetism, increasing the user's movement speed by 60% diminishing by 15% every second. If a basic attack is made while levitating, the user will fall to the ground forcefully, dealing damage to nearby enemies and knocking them up for 0.6s. This basic attack can lunge at a target crossing terrain and obstacles.

Upgrade (Level 13): While levitating, reduce incoming damage by 30%.

moves.name: Compute and Crush
moves.description: Has the user quickly analyze the position of nearby opposing Pokémon, granting the user a shield effect based on the number of opposing Pokémon analyzed. When 2 or fewer opposing Pokémon are analyzed, the user lunges toward the opposing Pokémon with the lowest HP, dealing damage to it. If an opposing Pokémon is knocked out by this move, the user recoves a portion of its Unite Move gauge When 3 or more opposing Pokémon are analyzed, the user lungest toward the opponent with the lowest HP, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon and creating a wall around itself.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Quickly analyzes the position of nearby enemies, granting a shield based on the number of enemies analyzed and become unstoppable. When 2 or fewer enemies are analyzed, lunge towards the enemy with the lowest HP, leaving it unable to act for 0.8s and dealing damage to it thrice, with the third hit dealing damage based on the target's missing HP. If an enemy is knocked out by this move, the user recovers 30% of its Unite Move gauge. If there are three or more analyzed, lunge toward the enemy with the lowest HP, dealing damage to nearby enemies and creating a wall around the user. If a wall is created, Compute and Crash may be used again to take down the wall.

20% Missing HP

passiveAbility.name: Clear Body
passiveAbility.description: Reduces the duration of hindrance effects by 10% when enemy players are nearby. The more enemy players there are, the more the hindrance effects are reduced, up to a maximum of 5 enemy players (50% max).
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: Reduces the duration of hindrance effects by 10% when enemy players are nearby. The more enemy players there are, the more the hindrance effects are reduced, up to a maximum of 5 enemy players (50% max).

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Each time Mew uses a move, they gain a boost counter. A maximum of 3 boost counters can be stored. After 3 counters are stored (they have to be consumed within 8s or they disappear), Mew's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack that consumes all counters and launches a ball of psychic energy at an enemy. During the lock-on animation Mew can use moves. The further the ball travels, the more damage it deals (4% more damage per threshold, stacking additively). This ball pierces through wild Pokémon, but will stop traveling upon contact with the first enemy player.

moves.name: Electro Ball
moves.description: Has the user hurl an electric orb, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and leaving them paralyzed for a short time. This move deals more damage the lower the opposing Pokémon's remaining HP is.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Hurl an electric orb, dealing damage to enemies in the area-of-effect and leaving them paralyzed (40% slow, 10% attack speed reduction) for 2s. This move deals more damage the lower the enemies' remaining HP is.

9.5% of enemy missing HP. Against Wild Pokemon: The execute damage is capped at 1200.

Through Light Screen: Damage dealt and area-of-effect radius are increased.

Upgrade (Level 10): Strengthens the effect of the paralysis inflicted by this move to 60% movement speed reduction and 30% attack speed reduction.

moves.name: Solar Beam
moves.description: Has the user blast a bundled beam of light, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. If this move hits any opposing Pokémon, the user gains one additional boost counter.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Blast a bundled beam of light, dealing damage 3 times to enemies in the area-of-effect. If this move hits an enemy, Mew gains an additional boost counter.

Through Light Screen: Damage dealt and area-of-effect radius are increased.

Upgrade (Level 10): Reduce this move's cooldown by 0.5s for each enemy hit.

moves.name: Surf
moves.description: Has the user charge forward on a wave, shoving opposing Pokémon and leaving them unable to act. If this move hits any opposing Pokémon, the user gains one additional boost counter. The user gains a shield and becomes immune to hindrances while using this move. At the end of this move, the user can jump in a designated direction and becomes immune to hindrances for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Charge forward on a wave, shoving enemies for 0.5s and leaving them unable to act for 0.5s. Mew gains a shield for 3s and becomes immune to hindrances while using this move. At the end of this move, Mew can jump in a designated direction and becomes immune to hindrances for a short time. If this move hits an enemy, Mew gains an additional boost counter.

Through Light Screen: Distance the wave travels is increased and Light Screen attaches to Mew.

Upgrade (Level 10): Strengthens the shield effect of this move.

moves.name: Coaching
moves.description: Has the user move toward an ally Pokémon, grant that Pokémon a shield, and increase that Pokémon's basic attack speed. Using this move on an ally Pokémon designates that ally as \u201ccoached\u201d (if it isn't already). If this move is used on an ally Pokémon that is not currently coached, this move's cooldown immediately resets. Coached ally Pokémon cease to be coached after a set amount of time. The cooldown of Electro Ball, Solar Beam, or Surf is reduced each time this move is used.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Teleport to an ally, granting them a shield for 3s and increasing basic attack speed by 40% for 2s. If this move is used on an ally who hasn't been coached within 7s, the cooldown of this move is reduced by 90%. If this move is used on an ally who has been recently coached, this move goes on cooldown. The cooldown of Electro Ball, Solar Beam, and Surf is reduced by 1s each time this move is used.

Upgrade (Level 12): Reduce this move's cooldown by 1s and reduces the timer of recently coached on allies to 6s.

moves.name: Light Screen
moves.description: Has the user create a translucent wall at the designated location. Opposing Pokémon cannot pass through the wall and will be shoved if they make contact with it. If the user's Electro Ball, Solar Beam, or Surf move passes through the wall, the effects of the move (such as damage, range, and area of effect) will be boosted. If this move is used again, it pulls the wall closer and has it move with the user. While the wall moves with the user, attacks from opposing Pokémon that pass through it will deal reduced damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Create a translucent wall in the designated location for 4s that prevents enemies from passing and shoves them for 0.5s if they make contact with the wall. If Mew's Electro Ball, Solar Beam, or Surf passes through the wall, the effects of those moves will be enhanced (such as increased damage, range, and area-of-effect). If this move is used again within 4s, the wall is pulled towards Mew and follows them as they move for an additional 4s. While the wall moves with Mew, enemy attacks that pass through it will deal 25% reduced damage but will not prevent enemies from passing and does not shove them anymore.

Upgrade (Level 12): Increases the size of the wall.

moves.name: Agility
moves.description: Has the user move quickly in the designated direction, increasing its movement speed for a short time. A maximum of two uses can be kept in reserve for this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Move quickly in a designated direction, increasing Mew's movement speed by 5% for 2s. Holds 2 uses in reserve and cannot be used more than once every 2s.

Upgrade (Level 12): Increases the distance Mew moves in the designated direction.

moves.name: Mystical Mirage
moves.description: Has the user float higher, create a field at its current location, and become invincible. The user and all ally Pokémon in the area of effect enter stealth. After a set amount of time passes, this Unite Move deals damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Mew becomes unstoppable and floats in the air for 1s, then becoming invincible and invisible for up to 6s until using an attack or move. A field is created around Mew for 5s This field travels with Mew. Mew and all allies within the area become stealthed for 4s. After the field expires, this move deals damage to enemies within the area-of-effect.

passiveAbility.name: Synchronize
passiveAbility.description: Each time Mew uses a move, Mew and nearby allies have their movement speed increased by 15% for 2s.

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: When Mewtwo Mega Evolves, deal increased damage and becomes a boosted attack with every third attack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: When Mewtwo Mega Evolves, deal increased damage and becomes a boosted attack with every third attack.

moves.name: Confusion
moves.description: Has the user deal damage to an opposing Pokémon and leave it unable to act for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Deal damage to an enemy and leave them unable to act for 1s.

moves.name: Future Sight
moves.description: Has the user pull the designated opposing Pokémon toward itself and lock on to it. The locked-on opposing Pokémon will have its movement speed decreased and take increased damage. After a set amount of time, this move deals damage to the locked-on opposing Pokémon based on the amount of damage it took while locked on.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mewtwo pulls the designated enemy toward itself dealing damage, leaving them unable to act for 0.75s and locking on to them. The locked-on enemy has their movement speed decreased by 25% for 3s and takes 10% increased damage from Mewtwo. After 3s, Mewtwo deals damage to the locked-on enemy a second time, and additional damage equal to 50% of any damage they received while locked-on.

50% of damage dealt while target is locked-on

Against Enemy Players: Maximum damage is capped at 2000.

Upgrade (Level 11): The locked-on enemy instead takes 20% increased damage.

moves.name: Psystrike
moves.description: Has the user direct psychic waves at the designated opposing Pokémon, dealing damage to it and decreasing its movement speed. While the user is directing psychic waves, it takes reduced damage. At the end of the move, the user hits the designated opposing Pokémon and nearby opposing Pokémon with a strong psychic wave, throwing and dealing damage to all opposing Pokémon it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mewtwo becomes unstoppable and starts directing psychic waves at the designated enemy, each hit dealing damage and decreasing their movement speed by 10% for 1s, stacking up to 5 times. While Mewtwo is directing psychic waves, it takes 15% reduced damage. At the end of the move, Mewtwo sends a final hit, dealing damage to the designated enemy and nearby enemies then throws them for 0.6s.

If Psystrike is intercepted by an enemy that isn't the intended target, there's no explosion and the enemy hit is shoved instead.

Upgrade (Level 11): Movement speed reduction on enemies is increased to 15% for 1s, stacking up to 5 times.

moves.name: Barrier
moves.description: Grants the user a shield for a short time. While the shield effect is active, the user's movement speed is increased.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mewtwo gains a shield for 3s. While the shield effect is active, its movement speed is increased by 20%.

10% Max HP

moves.name: Recover
moves.description: Grants the user a shield for a short time. While the shield effect is active, the user's movement speed is increased, and the user recovers HP based on the amount of damage it takes from opposing Pokémon. When the user takes enough damage from opposing Pokémon for the shield effect to be removed, all of the user's move cooldowns are reduced, and the Mega gauge increases.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mewtwo gains a shield for 3s. While the shield effect is active, movement speed is increased by 30%, and Mewtwo recovers HP equal to 20% of all incoming damage received from enemies while the shield is active. When Mewtwo takes enough damage for the shield effect to be removed, all of the user's move cooldowns are reduced by 25% of their current cooldown, and the Mega gauge increases twice.

10% Max HP

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the amount of HP recovered to 30% of incoming damage received.

moves.name: Teleport
moves.description: The user teleports to the designated location. For a short time, the user's movement speed and damage dealt increase, and its basic attacks increase the Mega gauge by a greater amount.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mewtwo teleports to the designated location. For 5s, movement speed is increased by 30%, Mewtwo deals 10% more damage, and the Mega gauge increases 50% faster.

Upgrade (Level 13): Damage dealt is increased to 20%.

moves.name: Infinite Psyburn
moves.description: Has the user bring down one powerful psychic blast upon each opposing Pokémon. Opposing Pokémon around each point of impact take damage, are left unable to act, and have their Defense decreased for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Mewtwo becomes unstoppable while revealing enemy players and channeling a powerful attack on the enemies' current position. After 1s, Mewtwo brings down a powerful psychic beam on their original position, dealing damage to enemies around each point of impact, leaving them unable to act for 1s and decreasing their Defense by 20% for 3s. Each Pokémon can only be hit by 1 psychic beam.

passiveAbility.name: Pressure
passiveAbility.description: When Mewtwo deals damage to an opposing Pokémon with a basic attack, the Mega gauge increases. Each time the Mega gauge increases, Mewtwo attack is increased by 2% and its Defense and Sp. Def are increased by 2%, up to 10 times. If Mewtwo is Lv. 5 or higher, and the Mega gauge has stacked at least 9 times, it Mega Evolves. The Mega Evolution lasts 15s and increases Mewtwo attack by an additional 10%, Defense and Sp. Def by an additional 10%, its max HP by 10% and its movement speed by 5%.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
When Mewtwo deals damage to enemy players with a basic attack, the Mega gauge increases up to 8 times. As the Mega gauge increases, Mewtwo's Attack, Defense, and Sp. Def are increased by 2%, up to 10 times. If Mewtwo is level 5 or higher, and the Mega gauge has increased at least 8 times, it Mega Evolves. The Mega Evolution lasts 10s and increases Mewtwo's Attack by an additional 18%, Defense and Sp. Def by an additional 18%, max HP by 10%, movement speed by 5%, and has a fixed attack speed.

Consumes up to 30 Aeos Energy upon Mega Evolving. Mega Evolution duration is extended by 1 second for every 5 energy consumed. Energy is consumed regardless if you have the next multiple of 5. The Mega gauge does not deplete upon being KO'd.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced: When Mewtwo Mega Evolves, attacks have increased range and deal damage in an area-of-effect centered on the target. When an attack deals damage to an enemy, their movement speed is decreased.

moves.name: Confusion
moves.description: Has the user deal damage to an opposing Pokémon and leave it unable to act for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Deal damage to an enemy and leave them unable to act for 1s.

moves.name: Future Sight
moves.description: Has the user shove the designated opposing Pokémon and lock on to it. The locked-on opposing Pokémon will have its movement speed decreased and take increased damage. After a set amount of time, this move deals damage to the locked-on opposing Pokémon based on the the amount of damage it took while locked on.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mewtwo shoves the designated enemy, dealing damage, leaving them unable to act for 0.5s and locking onto them. The locked-on enemy has their movement speed decreased by 35% for 3s and takes 10% increased damage from Mewtwo. After 3s, Mewtwo deals damage to the locked-on enemy a second time, and additional damage equal to 50% of any damage they received while locked-on.

Additional damage is capped at 1500.

Upgrade (Level 11): The locked-on enemy instead takes 15% increased damage.

moves.name: Psystrike
moves.description: Has the user direct psychic waves at the designated opposing Pokémon, dealing damage to it and decreasing its movement speed. At the end of the move, the user hits the designated opposing Pokémon and nearby opposing Pokémon with a strong psychic wave, throwing and dealing damage to all opposing Pokémon it hits. The farther the user is from the designated opposing Pokémon, the more damage this move deals.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mewtwo becomes unstoppable and starts directing psychic waves at the designated enemy, each hit dealing damage and decreasing their movement speed by 10% for 1s, stacking up to 5 times. Psystrike damage is increased based on distance from the target enemy; dealing up to 40% increased damage when Mewtwo is attacking from slightly beyond its basic attack range. At the end of the move, Mewtwo sends a final hit, dealing damage to the designated enemy and nearby enemies then throws them for 0.6s.

If Psystrike is intercepted by an enemy that isn't the intended target, there's no explosion and the enemy hit is shoved instead.

Upgrade (Level 11): Movement speed reduction on enemies is increased to 15% for 1s, stacking up to 5 times.

moves.name: Barrier
moves.description: Grants the user a shield for a short time. While the shield effect is active, the user's movement speed is increased.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mewtwo gains a shield for 3s. While the shield effect is active, its movement speed is increased by 20%.

10% max HP.

moves.name: Recover
moves.description: Grants the user a shield for a short time. While the shield effect is active, the user's movement speed is increased, and the user recovers HP based on the amount of damage it deals with basic attacks. When the shield effect is removed after a set amount of time, all of the user's move cooldowns are reduced, and the Mega gauge increases.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mewtwo gains a shield for 3s. While the shield effect is active, movement speed is increased by 30%, and Mewtwo recovers HP equal to 40% of the damage dealt from Basic Attacks. If at the end of the 3s the shield hasn't been destroyed, all of the user's move cooldowns are reduced by 25% of their current cooldown, and the Mega gauge increases once.

7.7% max HP.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the amount of HP recovered to 50% of damage dealt with Basic Attacks.

moves.name: Teleport
moves.description: The user teleports to the designated location. For a short time, the user's movement speed and damage dealt increases, and its basic attacks increase the Mega gauge by a greater amount. After using this move, for a short time, this move can be used again once only.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mewtwo teleports to the designated location. For 2s after the first cast of Teleport, movement speed is increased by 15%, Mewtwo deals 20% more damage, and the Mega gauge increases 50% faster. During those 2s, Mewtwo can teleport again once.

Upgrade (Level 13): Damage dealt is increased to 30%.

moves.name: Infinite Psyburn
moves.description: Has the user bring down one powerful psychic blast upon each opposing Pokémon. Opposing Pokémon around the point of impact take damage, are left unable to act, and have their Sp. Def decreased for a short time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Mewtwo becomes unstoppable while revealing enemy players and channeling a powerful attack on the enemies' current position. After 1s, Mewtwo brings down a powerful psychic beam on their original position, dealing damage to enemies around each point of impact, leaving them unable to act for 0.6s and decreasing their Sp. Defense by 15% for 3s. Each Pokémon can only be hit by 1 psychic beam.

passiveAbility.name: Pressure
passiveAbility.description: When Mewtwo deals damage to enemy players with a basic attack, the Mega gauge increases up to 12 times. As the Mega gauge increases, Mewtwo's Sp. Atk is increased by 1.5%, and attack speed is increased by 3%, up to 10 times. If Mewtwo is level 5 or higher, and the Mega gauge has increased at least 12 times, Mewtwo Mega Evolves. The Mega Evolution lasts 10s and increases Sp. Atk by an additional 10%, attack speed by an additional 35%, max HP by 10%, and movement speed by 5%.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
When Mewtwo deals damage to enemy players with a basic attack, the Mega gauge increases up to 12 times. As the Mega gauge increases, Mewtwo's Sp. Atk is increased by 1.5%, and attack speed is increased by 3%, up to 10 times. If Mewtwo is level 5 or higher, and the Mega gauge has increased at least 12 times, Mewtwo Mega Evolves. The Mega Evolution lasts 10s and increases Sp. Atk by an additional 10%, attack speed by an additional 35%, max HP by 10%, and movement speed by 5%.

Consumes up to 30 Aeos Energy upon Mega Evolving. Mega Evolution duration is extended by 1 second for every 5 energy consumed. Energy is consumed regardless if you have the next multiple of 5. The Mega gauge does not deplete upon being KO'd.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack or when using a move, striking twice with an increased area of attack.

moves.name: Astonish
moves.description: Has the user shout in a startling fashion, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Shout in a startling fashion, dealing damage to enemies hit.

moves.name: Play Rough
moves.description: Has the user jump forward, then move around opposing Pokémon it hits, dealing damage multiple times and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. After the attack is finished, the user jumps in the designated direction.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mimikyu dashes in a designated direction. If Mimikyu connects with an enemy, Mimikyu latches on, becoming invincible and follows the target, slowing them by 30% for 1s and dealing damage to them 5 times. Mimikyu will then leap off of the enemy in a designated direction. Hits after the first deal 70% reduced damage.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increases Mimikyu's movement speed by 20% for 3s after the attack is finished.

moves.name: Shadow Claw
moves.description: Has the user slash in front of itself with sharp claws, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits, decreasing their movement speed, and restoring the user's HP. Whenever the user hits opposing Pokémon with a basic attack, this move's cooldown is reduced, and the number of times the user attacks when this move is used next increases (maximum 4 times). The movement speed decreases caused by this move can stack.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mimikyu slashes in front of themself with sharp claws, dealing damage to enemies hit. Each hit decreases enemy movement speed by 20% for 1.5s, stacking up to 3 times, and restores the user's HP. Whenever Mimikyu hits at least one enemy with a basic or boosted attack, this move's cooldown is reduced by 1s, and the number of hits when this move is used again increases. Up to 4 of these additional hits can be stored and are lost after 5s without auto attacking or after using this move.

Upgrade (Level 11): At the end of the move, deal an additional hit of damage equal to 12% of missing HP. This damage is capped at 500 against wild Pokémon.

moves.name: Scratch
moves.description: Has the user charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. For a short time after the user charges, if this move is used again, the user attacks in front of itself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. At that time, additional damage is dealt based on the percentage of HP that the opposing Pokémon has lost.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mimikyu charges in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 2s. For 5s after using this move, Scratch can be used a second time, making Mimikyu attack in front of themself, dealing damage to enemies hit and additional damage based on their missing HP.

10% Missing HP

Additional damage is equal to 10% of the enemy's missing HP. Against Wild Pokemon: The total damage is capped at 500.

moves.name: Shadow Sneak
moves.description: Has the user extend its shadow, marking an opposing Pokémon it hits as a shadow target for a short time. If the shadow passes through tall grass or a wall, it extends even further. If the user deals damage to an opposing Pokémon marked as a shadow target, additional damage is dealt based on the percentage of HP that the opposing Pokémon has lost. If this move is used again, the user moves to an opposing Pokémon marked as a shadow target, deals damage to it, and extends the duration of that Pokémon's shadow mark. If the user knocks out a Pokémon marked as a shadow target, this move's cooldown is reset and the user recovers HP.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mimikyu moves backwards a short distance and extends their shadow, marking an enemy hit as a shadow target for 3s. If the shadow passes through tall grass or a wall, the shadow extends even further. If Mimikyu deals damage to an enemy who is marked as a shadow target, each hit dealt will deal additional damage equal to 4% of the target's missing HP. If this move is used again, Mimikyu moves to the enemy marked as a shadow target, fears them for 0.25s, deals damage to enemies in an area, and refresh the duration of that enemy's shadow mark. If the marked target gets knocked out Shadow Sneak's cooldown is reset and Mimikyu recovers HP.

4% Missing HP

10% Max HP

Against Wild Pokemon: The % missing HP damage is capped at 150.

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

moves.name: Trick Room
moves.description: Has the user create a bizarre area around itself. If a movement speed decrease is applied to the user while it is within the area, that effect is converted into a movement speed increase, and the user takes reduced damage from Pokémon outside the area. Also, while in the area, the user can use this move again to move a set distance forward, as often as desired.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Leap to a location and create a bizarre area around Mimikyu in a square for 6s. If a slow would be applied to Mimikyu within the area, Mimikyu instead receives a movement speed increase equal to the slow rate. While inside the Trick Room, Mimikyu receives 50% reduced damage from enemies outside of the Trick Room. This move can be used as often as desired while inside the Trick Room, teleporting Mimikyu a set distance with each use.

Upgrade (Level 13): Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

moves.name: Play With Me...
moves.description: Has the user leap at an opposing Pokémon and trap it inside its rag, dealing damage over time and granting the user a shield. The trapped opposing Pokémon isn't affected by any Pokémon's moves except the user's. If the user loses its shield, or if a set amount of time passes, the user stops attacking and jumps in the designated direction. Shadow Claw Effect: Number of attacks increased. Disguise Effect: Nullifying damage for a set amount of time.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mimikyu leaps at an enemy and traps them inside Mimikyu's rag, dealing damage over time and granting the user a shield. The trapped enemy is only affected by this Unite Move and effects applied by Mimikyu before using this Unite Move. If Mimikyu loses their shield, or if 2.8s passes, the user stops attacking and jumps in the designated direction. The maximum number of hits is 7, hitting every 0.4s. The Unite buffs occur after the move ends. This ability counters Unstoppable.

15% Max HP

passiveAbility.name: Disguise
passiveAbility.description: Mimikyu starts the game in Disguised Form. While in Disguised Form, when Mimikyu would take damage from an enemy, Mimikyu instead takes 5% remaining HP as piercing damage, becomes Impervious for 0.5s (immune to damage but not hindrances) and changes to Busted Form. A revenge mark is applied on the enemy that broke the disguise. Mimikyu's movement speed increases by 10% when approaching revenge-marked enemies and deals 10% increased damage to them. After 50s in Busted Form, Mimikyu returns to Disguised Form. The time it takes to return to Disguised Form is reduced by 25s when Mimikyu knocks out an enemy player and is fully reset upon respawning.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: Mimikyu starts the game in Disguised Form. While in Disguised Form, when Mimikyu would take damage from an enemy, Mimikyu instead takes 5% remaining HP as piercing damage, becomes Impervious for 0.5s (immune to damage but not hindrances) and changes to Busted Form. A revenge mark is applied on the enemy that broke the disguise. Mimikyu's movement speed increases by 10% when approaching revenge-marked enemies and deals 10% increased damage to them. After 50s in Busted Form, Mimikyu returns to Disguised Form. The time it takes to return to Disguised Form is reduced by 25s when Mimikyu knocks out an enemy player and is fully reset upon respawning.

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Miraidon does not have a boosted attack.

moves.name: Thunder Shock
moves.description: Has the user send a jolt of electricity in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Fire a jolt of electricity in a line in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit.

moves.name: Charge Beam
moves.description: Has the user charge power before firing an electric beam in the designated direction. If this move hits opposing Pokémon, the user's Sp. Atk is increased for a short time, and the user stores electric power. If the user stores electric power twice within a short time, it can use a boosted version of Charge Beam. The boosted version has the user continuously fire an electric beam in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. A maximum of 2 use(s) can be kept in reserve for this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Charge power before firing an electric beam in a designated direction. If this move hits an enemy, Miraidon's Sp. Atk is increased by 10% for 6s and stores electric power, stacking twice. If electric power is stored twice within a short time, Charge Beam becomes Boosted. Boosted Charge Beam can be held for up to 10s and continuously fires an electric beam in a designated direction, dealing damage to all enemies in the area of effect and granting Miraidon Hindrance Resistance. Miraidon may move while firing the electric beam but may not change the original direction. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve. There is a 1s cooldown between uses.

Upgrade (Level 11): Reduces the cooldown to charge by 0.5s.

moves.name: Electro Drift
moves.description: Has the user race forward in the designated direction while spinning, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in its path. The user also stores electric power. If the user stores electric power twice within a short time, it can use a boosted version of Electro Drift. The boosted version has the user do a spinning attack aimed at the designated opposing Pokémon, dealing damage to any opposing Pokémon it hits. This move deals increased damage if the opposing Pokémon's remaining HP is low. A maximum of 2 use(s) can be kept in reserve for this move.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Race forward while spinning, dealing damage to enemies in the path. If this move hits an enemy, Miraidon stores electric power. If electric power is stored twice within a short time, Electro Drift becomes Boosted. Boosted Electro Drift can be held for up to 10s and homes in on a designated enemy with the spinning attack, dealing damage to any enemy hit. This move deals increased damage instead if the remaining HP of the targets hit are below 50%. During this move, Miraidon is Hindrance Resistant. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve. There is a 1s cooldown between uses.

Upgrade (Level 11): Reduces the cooldown to charge by 4s.

moves.name: Thunder Wave
moves.description: Has the user discharge electricity at the designated opposing Pokémon, dealing damage and leaving them paralyzed.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Discharges electricity at a designated enemy, dealing damage and leaving them paralyzed, decreasing attack speed by 10% and movement speed by 30% for 2s.

moves.name: Thunder
moves.description: Has the user summon a cloud in the skies above that drops wicked thunderbolts. The summoned cloud moves slowly toward the nearest opposing Pokémon, dropping thunderbolts whenever a set amount of time passes. The thunderbolts deal damage and apply a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon they hit.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Summon a cloud in the skies dropping thunderbolts at set intervals. The summoned cloud moves slowly towards the nearest enemy. Thunderbolts deal damage and apply a 10% slow to enemies hit.

Upgrade (Level 13): Increases the slow effect to 20%.

moves.name: Parabolic Charge
moves.description: Has the user flood the area around it with electricity, dealing damage and applying a slowing effect to opposing Pokémon it hits. If this move hits opposing Pokémon, the user recovers HP and also recovers 1 stored use(s) of either Charge Beam or Electro Drift. The more opposing Pokémon the move hits, the more HP the user recovers.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Floods the area around Miraidon with electricity, dealing damage and applying a 50% slow to enemies hit for 2s. If this move hits an enemy, Miraidon recovers HP and 1 stored use of either Charge Beam or Electro Drift. The more targets hit, the more HP Miraidon recovers.

Upgrade (Level 13): Grants a shield when this move is used.

moves.name: Bright Future Meteor Storm
moves.description: Has the user call down 5 meteorites in a row, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon they hit. If a meteorite hits an opposing Pokémon, the next meteorite to fall will deal increased damage. The final meteorite deals higher damage in a wider area of effect and throws opponents.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user gains hindrance resistance and calls down up to 5 meteorites in a row, each at a designated area, damaging opposing Pokémon hit by each meteorite. If a meteorite hits an opposing Pokémon, it increases the damage of subsequent meteorites that hit by an additional 20% (up to 80% additional increased damage for the final meteorite if all preceding meteorites hit). The final meteorite damages opposing Pokémon in a wider area of effect and throws them for 1s when it hits.

All effects related to Unite Move use activate when the first meteorite is called down. This Unite Move ends when released, consuming 10% of the Unite Move gauge. Each meteorite called down consumes at most 30% of the Unite Move gauge.

passiveAbility.name: Hadron Engine
passiveAbility.description: Create Electric Terrain around Miraidon for 10s when hitting an enemy player with a Move. If Miraidon has Aeos Energy, up to 40 energy (in multiples of 10) will be consumed when the Electric Terrain is created, increasing the size of the Electric Terrain. Miraidon and Pokémon cannot be put to sleep on Electric Terrain. Miraidon on the Electric Terrain deals 30% additional Move damage while allies will deal 10% additional Move damage. Electric Terrain also strengthens ally team goals' healing and shielding by 30% and weakens enemy team goals' healing and shielding by 30%. This ability goes on a 30s cooldown after it's triggered.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced: Create Electric Terrain around Miraidon for 10s when hitting an enemy player with a Move. If Miraidon has Aeos Energy, up to 40 energy (in multiples of 10) will be consumed when the Electric Terrain is created, increasing the size of the Electric Terrain. Miraidon and Pokémon cannot be put to sleep on Electric Terrain. Miraidon on the Electric Terrain deals 30% additional Move damage while allies will deal 10% additional Move damage. Electric Terrain also strengthens ally team goals' healing and shielding by 30% and weakens enemy team goals' healing and shielding by 30%. This ability goes on a 30s cooldown after it's triggered.

builds.name: Thunder Beam
builds.emblemName: Special Attack Cooldown Reduction - Mobility for Survivability

moves.name: Attack
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Moltres strikes opponents with their beak. Becomes a boosted attack every fourth attack, swiping with their flaming wing. Once a boosted attack hits a target, the user gains one free move use for 3s. After this move is used or the duration expires, Moltres gains 600 unite energy. This unite energy can be boosted by Energy Amp and other similar effects.

moves.name: Wing Attack
moves.description: Has the user spread its wings and charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user dashes in the designated direction, dealing damage along the path.

moves.name: Sky Attack
moves.description: Has the user spread its wings wide and attack with a powered-up dash in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon. When this move hits Pokémon from the opposing team, then for a short time, the more Pokémon from the opposing team the move hits, the more the user's Defense and Sp. Def are increased.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user dashes in the designated direction, dealing damage along the path. Each hit increases the user's Def and SpDef by 8% for 4s, stacking up to 5 times.

Upgrade (Level 13): Grants a shield on a successful hit for 3s.

moves.name: Incinerate
moves.description: Has the user hurl a fiery orb in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon. The higher the burn counter of the opposing Pokémon it hits, the more damage it deals. When this move hits an opposing Pokémon, that Pokémon's burn counter resets.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: The user hurls a fiery orb in the designated direction, dealing damage to all opponents hit.

moves.name: Heat Wave
moves.description: Has the user exhale hot breath in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and gradually restoring the user's HP for a short time. The higher the burn counter of the opposing Pokémon it hits, the more damage it deals. When this move hits an opposing Pokémon, that Pokémon's burn counter resets.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user exhales hot breath in the designated direction, dealing damage and granting health regeneration. This health regeneration lasts 2s and ticks every 0.5s.

5% of Max HP

Upgrade (Level 11): Lowers the cooldown of this move by 1s for each Pokémon from the opposing team hit.

moves.name: Firebird Inferno
moves.description: Has the user soar upward and unleash a blazing orb of fire toward the designated area, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and maximising their burn counter. After this move is used, the area around the opponent's scorable goal zones will become visible to the user.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
The user becomes Unstoppable and soars upwards before unleashing a blazing orb towards the designated area, dealing damage, applying 5 ticks of burn damage instantly, and 5 stacks of burn counter to all enemies hit.

For 20s afterwards, a modest area around the opposing team's goal zones are revealed. Moltres cannot gain unite energy from KO'ing wild Pokémon or scoring.

passiveAbility.name: Flame Body
passiveAbility.description: Whenever the user deals damage with a Basic Attack, Boosted Attack, Wing Attack, or Sky Attack, the target is left with a burn and a burn counter that caps at 5 stacks. The burn and burn counter lasts 4s and deals damage every 0.5s.
passiveAbility.descriptionAdvanced:
Whenever the user deals damage with a Basic Attack, Boosted Attack, Wing Attack, or Sky Attack, the target is left with a burn and a burn counter that caps at 5 stacks. The burn and burn counter lasts 4s and deals damage every 0.5s.

If Incinerate or Heat Wave are used, the burn is cleansed and the burn counter is consumed, increasing the damage of those moves by 10% per burn counter stack.

moves.name: Attack
moves.description: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage and throwing enemies when it hits.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Gains a Boosted Attack every 5s, dealing increased damage and throwing enemies in the air for 0.5s.

moves.name: Fake Out
moves.description: Slap its hands together, shoving enemies in front of it.
moves.descriptionAdvanced: Mr. Mime claps, damaging and knocking back enemies hit for 0.4s.

moves.name: Confusion
moves.description:
Attacks straight in front of the user, dealing damage to enemies and shoving them. If an enemy shoved by this move hits an obstacle, it receives increased damage from this move and is left unable to act for a short time. If the enemy is currently guard-swapped, it is not shoved but receives increased damage from this move and is left unable to act for a short time.

Upgrade (Level 11): Increased damage.
moves.descriptionAdvanced:
Mr. Mime fires a wave of psychic energy, damaging and knocking enemies back. Enemies hit into a wall take bonus damage and are stunned for 1.5s. Enemies debuffed by Power Swap take bonus damage and are stunned for 0.7s, but are not knocked back.

Normal damage will apply to targets that are unstoppable or unstunnable. (Example: Drednaw & Zapdos)

Upgrade (Level 11): Increased damage.

moves.name: Psychic
moves.description:
